title: [bug] New Chat overwrites the single saved session — thread id reused across chats
description: User repro (post PUNK-hdd fix): hitting + nukes the previous chat session; only ONE chat session is ever saved on disk. Symptom signature: every persist writes the same thread file → the active thread id is not rotating across New Chat, or the + affordance clears messages without starting a genuinely fresh thread. Investigate ChatThread()/startFreshThread/newChat/persistActiveThread id lifecycle and what each + button actually invokes.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple sequential chats produce multiple thread files and list entries; + never overwrites a previous session; disk state verified across several new-chat cycles; regression coverage where feasible
notes: DIAGNOSIS COMPLETE — data is safe. Evidence: (1) user's vault ~/Documents/Vaults/.punkrecords/threads contains ALL 10 conversations incl. 4 written during the failing retest (17:02-17:08 Jul 6) — persistence has been working; (2) new --chat-persistence-selftest harness driving the REAL app binary passes on a fresh vault AND lists all 10 threads from a copy of the user's real data; (3) all 10 threads are parentless — no tree-assembly involvement; (4) discovered THREE nested vaults each with own .punkrecords (~/Documents/Vaults parent + Research Vault + ghost-trilogy-videos); ONLY the parent has threads. Leading explanation for 'empty after restart': the restarted session opened a NESTED vault (or restoration restored a different vault window) whose chat list is legitimately empty. Remaining unexplained: 'only one visible' mid-session — not reproducible against current main with real data. Awaiting user confirmation on a fresh build of current main with ~/Documents/Vaults (the parent) explicitly opened.

title: [bug] Active chat missing from thread list; New Chat (+) erases current conversation
description: User repro: created a chat, used it, opened the thread list — the active chat was NOT listed. Hit the + (New Chat) button and the current conversation was erased. Suspected area: ChatSessionController persist/summary-refresh ordering after the PUNK-hv3/PUNK-9ss changes (newChat defers writing empty threads until content exists; threadSummaries may not refresh after the first persist; switching away from a never-persisted thread would drop its messages). Data-loss severity: determine whether the thread JSON reaches disk before the list goes stale.
acceptance_criteria: Active thread with content always appears in the sidebar/thread list; New Chat never destroys an unsaved conversation (persist-before-switch); regression tests cover first-message persist + summary refresh + newChat ordering; root cause documented
close_reason: Root cause: ChatSessionController's thread store was only wired by view .tasks; a same-vault reopen replaced the shared controller AFTER those tasks fired (openVault recreated it unconditionally; panel .task had no id → never re-fired), so persistActiveThread's 'guard let store' silently dropped every save, the list stayed empty, and newChat destroyed the only copy. Fixed: ensureThreadStore on-demand wiring at every entry point (concurrent callers await shared wiring task); persist surfaces a visible error when unwired; newChat/switchTo persist-before-clear; openVault reuses the wired controller on same-vault reopen; panel task keyed to controller identity. Gates green (128 integration + 24 eval, strict lint 0, arch OK). Automated regression blocked by SPM link failure when app-hosting the test bundle — PUNK-ozb tracks coordinator extraction + the three regression scenarios.

title: Session path sends empty text block to Anthropic — all live session turns 400
description: Every LiveSessionAgentEvals scenario against Anthropic fails: HTTP 400 'messages: text content blocks must be non-empty'. The legacy AgentLoop live evals pass, so this is session-path-specific — almost certainly SessionAgentRunner passing instructions: '' to LanguageModelSession, which ALM's AnthropicLanguageModel serializes as an empty text content block (its transcript-based message rebuild, cf. upstream #156, includes the empty instructions entry). This means the app's shipping Anthropic chat path is broken. Fix: never materialize an empty instructions entry (omit instructions when empty in the runner; verify ALM's serialization), then re-run LiveSessionAgentEvals to confirm. Found by the eval baseline run during PUNK-k3i.
acceptance_criteria: LiveSessionAgentEvals Anthropic scenarios pass with a real key; no empty text blocks in serialized requests.
notes: Two root causes, both empty text blocks in ALM AnthropicLanguageModel serialization: (1) instructions:'' materializes an .instructions transcript entry -> empty user text block (fixed: session constructed without instructions); (2) LanguageModelSession.respond appends a .response transcript entry even when a tool-only round returns no text -> empty assistant text block poisons every subsequent round (fixed: fresh LanguageModelSession per round — the runner already re-folds instructions+toolLog into each round's prompt, so the transcript was redundant context duplication anyway). search+synthesize passed pre-fix only because Claude narrated alongside its tool_use.
close_reason: Committed on foundationmodels-rehoming: offline efficacy evals ported to session engine (38 green), Anthropic empty-block 400 fixed via fresh-session-per-round (live session evals 4/4), deployment target restored to macOS 26

title: [bug] Sidebar renders nested folders as flat full-path rows instead of a recursive tree
description: User screenshot evidence: vault sidebar shows each distinct folder PATH as its own top-level disclosure row labeled with the full path (blackwork-vault/code/blackwork/nod..., Research Vault/Research Digests, ...) instead of nesting subfolders under their parents. Expected: a strict recursive tree — blackwork-vault is ONE top-level node containing code/, experiments/, notes/, references/, with code/ containing blackwork/ etc., exactly like single-level folders (Chiba) already appear. Root cause: SidebarFilter.filter produces flat [SidebarFolderGroup] keyed by full folder path and VaultBrowserView renders one top-level DisclosureGroup per group with the raw path as label — no recursion. Fix: pure Core tree assembly over the folder groups (mirror the ChatThreadHelpers.threadTree precedent), recursive folder rows in the view, sensible label = last path component. Decide + document filtering interplay (filtered results must remain discoverable — e.g. auto-expand matching branches or keep flat groups only while a filter is active). Preserve selection binding, rename, delete, context menus, and root-level files at top.
acceptance_criteria: Unfiltered sidebar shows strict nesting (folder labels are single path components, subfolders inside parents); deep vaults like blackwork-vault render as one tree; filtering still surfaces matches; selection/rename/delete/context menus work at every depth; tree assembly is a pure tested Core function incl. deep nesting, siblings ordering, and files-at-every-level cases; existing suites green
close_reason: Sidebar now renders a strict recursive folder tree: SidebarFilter.folderTree assembles SidebarFolderNode nesting from the existing flat groups (doc-less intermediate folders synthesized, case-insensitive sibling sort, root docs stay flat above the tree); recursive FolderTreeRow renders it with per-branch expansion state; labels are last path components. Filtering auto-expands the match-pruned tree (ancestors of matches by construction). Selection/rename/delete/context menus preserved at every depth. 13 new tree tests + 13 existing filter tests green in the integration bundle (140/140), 414 Core tests, strict lint 0, arch OK. Merged as cc21f85. Visual nesting appearance manual-only.

title: [bug] Chats section header: + hidden behind hover, collides with disclosure triangle
description: User repro: the sidebar Chats section puts a + button next to the section's disclosure triangle; macOS hover-reveals sidebar section accessories, so both the + and the chevron vanish until mouse-over (hidden affordance — usability/discoverability no-no) and hovering toggles the other + out of view. New Chat needs an always-visible affordance: e.g. a fixed 'New Chat' row inside the section (standard list row, always rendered) instead of a header accessory button.
acceptance_criteria: New Chat affordance is always visible without hover; no accessory sits next to the section disclosure triangle; section collapse still works; a11y ids preserved/updated
close_reason: New Chat moved from Section header accessory (hover-hidden next to disclosure chevron by macOS) to an always-visible plus.circle row at the top of the Chats section; header is plain 'Chats'. sidebarNewChatButton a11y id preserved. Gates green (128 integration + 20 eval, strict lint 0, arch OK).

title: Extract chat thread-lifecycle orchestration into a package-testable coordinator (PUNK-hdd regression coverage)
description: The PUNK-hdd data-loss fix (ensureThreadStore on-demand wiring, persist-before-clear in newChat/switchTo, controller reuse on same-vault reopen, controller-identity-keyed panel task) lives in App-layer ChatSessionController orchestration, which has no automated regression coverage: App target has no unit bundle, and making PunkRecordsIntegrationTests app-hosted (TEST_HOST/BUNDLE_LOADER + target dep) breaks the SPM build graph — AnyLanguageModel's dynamic package framework fails to link OrderedCollections (undefined nominal type descriptor) once the test bundle depends on the app target; clean rebuild did not help. Per CLAUDE.md discipline, the right fix: extract the thread-lifecycle orchestration (store wiring, persist/switch/new/delete/fork ordering, summaries refresh) into a coordinator in Core (store factory injected; no AppKit) so it's swift-test-able with a mock ThreadStore, and slim ChatSessionController to UI glue. Regression tests to port from the deleted Tests/PunkRecordsIntegrationTests/ChatThreadPersistenceRegressionTests.swift (in git history at PUNK-hdd's commit): persist-wires-on-demand + list visibility, newChat-persists-before-clearing, same-vault-reopen keeps wired coordinator.
acceptance_criteria: Thread lifecycle orchestration is a Core (or Infra) type tested without the app module; the three PUNK-hdd regression scenarios have automated tests; ChatSessionController delegates to it; existing suites green
close_reason: Merged to main (175abf1), validated by orchestrator: thread lifecycle extracted to Core ChatThreadCoordinator (@MainActor @Observable; store factory/focusNote/reportError injected as closures, unowned appState matching pre-existing controller semantics — verified no new crash window). ChatSessionController slimmed ~100 lines to UI glue with computed forwards. 8 new coordinator tests incl. the PUNK-b51 regression (N newChat cycles → N distinct persisted threads). Note: the deleted ChatThreadPersistenceRegressionTests.swift was NOT recoverable from git history (agent checked mainline/branches/reflog/dangling); scenarios rewritten fresh from the issue text. Post-merge validation: 466 Core tests, 140 integration tests, build, swiftlint --strict all green.

title: Sidebar Threads section with focus-note visibility and jump affordance
description: Implement the chosen thread-switcher direction (PUNK-sd3 study): a first-class Chats section in the main window sidebar, replacing the dropdown Menu as the primary thread surface. Requires the one-time hoist: thread state (threadSummaries, activeThreadID, switchTo/newChat/deleteThread) moves out of LLMChatPanel's private ChatSessionController @State into an AppState-reachable source of truth shared by sidebar and panel. Sidebar rows: title, relative date, branch nesting for forks (DisclosureGroup via parentThreadID added additively to ThreadSummary with lenient decode), active thread highlighted, context menu (delete; new chat affordance in the section header), selecting a thread activates it AND reveals the chat panel. USER REQUIREMENT 1: each thread row clearly signifies the note the chat was focusing on (focus note = most recent message context in the thread; derive via pure Core function; persist on ChatThread/summary additively so the sidebar shows it without loading bodies; old schemaVersion-1 files must decode leniently). USER REQUIREMENT 2: after switching to a thread, a clear affordance to open its focus note — panel header shows active thread title (fixing the static 'Chats' label) plus a tappable focus-note chip that opens that note. Header Menu slims to actions only (Summarize etc.) since the list lives in the sidebar. Sidebar selection model: resolve the single selectedDocumentPath binding (separate section selection or unified enum — tested).
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar shows a Chats section: all threads, active highlighted, forks nested, each row showing its focus note; clicking activates the thread and opens the chat panel; panel header shows the thread title + tappable focus-note chip that opens the note; thread state hoisted to a single shared source of truth; old thread files decode; focus-note derivation, tree building, and selection logic are pure tested Core functions; existing chat/thread/integration suites stay green
close_reason: Sidebar Chats section shipped per PUNK-sd3 decision + user requirements: AppState-owned shared ChatSessionController (single store/migration, reentrancy-guarded load), collapsible sidebar section with fork nesting (orphan/cycle-safe threadTree), active-thread highlight, row focus-note subtitles (ChatNoteContext.focusNote — newest resolvable note context, vault-wide turns skipped, persisted in thread header so summaries stay body-free, lenient decode proven with old-format fixture), selection activates thread + reveals panel. Panel header shows active thread title + tappable chatThreadFocusNote chip opening the note; menu slimmed to actions. Validated on merged main: 128 integration + 29 eval + 414 Core/217 Infra (agent run) green; strict lint 0; arch OK. ff-merged 300763c. Visuals + newest-thread-active-on-open behavior flagged for manual QA.

title: Thread switcher UX: evaluate alternatives to the dropdown menu
description: User feedback: the thread switcher dropdown Menu in LLMChatPanel is not the right UX. Design-analysis task: survey alternative mechanisms for surfacing threads (sidebar/source-list, palette, popover list, inspector, tabs, etc.), evaluate pros/cons against this app's architecture (ThreadSummary with hasParent fork lineage, multi-window vaults, existing QuickOpen/search palette precedents), rank, and present the top 3 for a human decision. Implementation happens in a follow-up once a direction is chosen.
acceptance_criteria: A ranked proposal with pros/cons for each candidate and a clearly argued top 3, grounded in the actual codebase (current switcher implementation, panel layout, summaries API); user picks a direction
notes: Opus design study complete. Ranking: 1) Sidebar Threads section (glanceable, only tree-capable option, M-L), 2) Quick-switcher palette cloning QuickOpenView (fastest keyboard switch, S-M), 3) Header popover (cheapest Menu upgrade, no state hoist, S-M). Rejected: in-panel column (panel is only 300-350pt wide — crushes both sides), tab strip (caps at ~5-6 threads, no lineage), hover drawer (hidden affordance, untestable). Key architectural finding: ChatSessionController is private @State inside LLMChatPanel, so any mechanism OUTSIDE the panel (sidebar/palette) requires hoisting threadSummaries/activeThread/switchTo into AppState — one hoist unlocks both #1 and #3. Recommended: hybrid #1+#3 after one hoist; optionally ship #4 popover as immediate stopgap. Near-free win: show active thread title in the panel header (currently static 'Chats'). Awaiting user direction.
close_reason: Decision: option 1 — sidebar Threads section. User added two requirements: (1) thread rows must clearly signify the note the chat was focusing on; (2) after switching to a thread, an affordance to jump to that focus note. Implementation ticket follows.

title: KB-wide search UI: first-class user search over the same index the agent uses
description: Users currently have Quick Open (filename matching) but no first-class full-text search, while the agent gets FTS5/BM25 search with tag:/title: filters via VaultSearchTool (SQLiteSearchIndex, filters fixed in PUNK-ilq). Give users the same power: a vault-wide search interface (e.g. dedicated panel or sidebar mode, ⌘⇧F) over the SAME SearchService: as-you-type (debounced) BM25-ranked results with title + highlighted snippet, full query syntax support (free text, tag:<tag>, title:<text>, combinations), result count, keyboard navigation (arrows + return), click/return opens the note — ideally scrolling to/highlighting the first match. Empty/no-results states with a syntax hint. Per CLAUDE.md discipline: query→display-model mapping, snippet highlight-range computation, and keyboard-navigation state are pure tested Core functions (mirror QuickOpenMatcher precedent); the view is a thin shell. Reuse the existing snippet/highlight machinery in SQLiteSearchIndex/SearchResult if present — check what the agent-facing results already carry before building new. Keep Quick Open (filename) as-is and distinct.
acceptance_criteria: ⌘⇧F (or equivalent) opens vault-wide search; typing shows debounced BM25 results with highlighted snippets; tag:/title: syntax works identically to the agent tool (parity asserted in tests against SearchQueryParser semantics); keyboard navigation + open-on-return; selected result opens the note and scrolls to the first match where feasible; empty/no-result states present; display-model mapping, highlight ranges, and list navigation state are pure unit-tested Core functions; integration test drives an indexed TempVault through the search service to the display model
close_reason: Vault-wide search sheet on ⌘⇧F (Filter Sidebar moved to ⌘⌥⇧F): debounced as-you-type BM25 results with FTS5 <mark>-parsed highlight snippets, full tag:/title: syntax, keyboard nav, open-on-return. Agent↔UI parity test proves both paths share SearchService semantics. Pure Core VaultSearchDisplay (14 tests) + 8 integration parity tests; 107 integration tests green on merged main; strict lint + arch check OK. Scroll-to-match deliberately deferred (no editor reveal hook); panel visuals manual-only.

title: Show selected-note context per chat message: in UI, to the LLM, and in the thread schema
description: When a chat turn runs with a selected note (chat scope / MessageContext), that association must be visible everywhere: (1) SCHEMA — each persisted thread message serializes its MessageContext (selected note id/title + scope) so history is faithful across restarts; coordinated with PUNK-hv3 whose ChatThread schema must round-trip ChatMessage.context. (2) UI — each user message with note context shows a small context chip (note title, clickable to open the note); the composer area shows the CURRENT context ('Chatting about: <note>') so the user knows what the next turn will include. (3) LLM — the turn prompt explicitly names the selected note (e.g. 'The user has selected the note titled X (path Y)' via ContextBuilder instructions) rather than only inlining its content anonymously; verify what ContextBuilder emits today and make the selection explicit. Chip rendering data + prompt phrase construction are pure tested functions.
acceptance_criteria: Persisted threads round-trip message context; reloaded threads show the same context chips; composer displays current note context; prompt contains an explicit named reference to the selected note (asserted via PromptLog/ScriptedLanguageModel eval); chip data + prompt fragment are pure tested Core functions
close_reason: Selected-note context now visible everywhere: per-message chatContextChip (doc.text + title, opens note, works for reloaded threads via persisted MessageContext), chatContextBanner 'Chatting about: <title>' at composer, and ContextBuilder explicitly names the selected note in instructions (note-scope only; vault-wide prompts byte-identical). Pure ChatNoteContext Core decision (11 tests) + 2 ContextBuilder tests + deterministic SelectedNoteInstructionEvals via PromptLog; SessionContextThreadingEvals invariants stay green. Validated on merged main: 124 integration + 25 eval tests, strict lint 0, arch OK. Merged as 51a5c81; chip/banner visuals manual-only.

title: Summarize conversation into a note, asking where to save afterward
description: Add 'Summarize to Note' to the chat panel (toolbar/thread menu). Flow: run a summarization request over the active thread's transcript through the existing session path (active provider; compact transcript rendering as input — coordinate with PUNK-z5s which needs the same pure transcript-rendering helper; build it once in Core). While summarizing, the normal waiting indicator applies. When the summary is ready, present a save dialog asking WHERE to save: destination folder within the vault + editable note title (prefilled from thread title, e.g. 'Summary — <thread title>'); on confirm, create the note via the repository following frontmatter conventions, then open it. Cancel discards nothing — offer retry/copy. Pure tested pieces: transcript-to-prompt rendering, default title derivation, suggested filename/path derivation (VaultPaths conventions). Depends on threads (PUNK-hv3) for thread title + transcript access.
acceptance_criteria: Menu action produces an LLM summary of the full active conversation via the session path; save sheet appears AFTER summarization with folder picker + prefilled title; confirming creates a proper frontmatter note at the chosen path and opens it; cancel keeps the summary available (copy/retry); transcript rendering, title, and path derivation unit-tested; scripted-model eval covers the summarize turn deterministically
close_reason: Summarize-to-Note shipped: ConversationSummarizer Core actor over the TextCompleter seam (one-shot, active provider via DeferredSessionTextCompleter), reuses ThreadTranscriptRenderer with ProviderRegistry contextBudget. Structured faithful-summary prompt (Topic/Key Points/Decisions/Open Questions). Save sheet appears AFTER summary: folder picker + 'Summary — <thread title>' prefill, collision-safe filename, frontmatter note created + opened. Cancel → Retry/Copy/Discard fallback. 13 Core tests + 4 deterministic end-to-end evals via ScriptedLanguageModel + TempVaultFactory. Final gate on merged main: 124 integration + 29 eval + 398 Core tests, strict lint 0, arch OK. Merged as 68ee42e; sheet visuals + interactive flow manual-only.

title: Chat waiting indicator: animated typing bubble while awaiting model response
description: While waiting for the model (request sent, no assistant text yet — including gaps after tool-call chips before the next text), the chat shows an assistant-style bubble with an animated three-dot ellipsis so users know nothing is wrong. Indicator appears when a turn is in flight and the latest visible content is not actively streaming text; disappears on first streamed text or turn end/error. The show/hide decision should be a pure function over the controller/reducer state (tested); the animation itself is a small SwiftUI view (visuals manual). Respect reduced-motion (static ellipsis).
acceptance_criteria: Indicator visible between send and first assistant text and between tool chip and following text; hides on first text token, completion, or error; show/hide decision unit-tested; reduced-motion honored; no layout jump when replaced by the real bubble
close_reason: ChatWaitingIndicator.shouldShow pure Core decision (9 tests: send gap, tool-chip gaps, streaming text hides, end/error hides) + ChatWaitingIndicatorView (staggered 3-dot animation, Reduce Motion static fallback, chatWaitingIndicator a11y id, bubble chrome matched — no layout jump). 5-line LLMChatPanel diff. Build + 20 eval-bundle tests + 99 integration + strict lint + arch check green in agent run; ff-merged as 523fc54. Animation feel is manual-only.

title: Thread forking: branch a conversation from any message
description: With threads persisted (see thread-store issue), allow forking: from any message in the active thread, 'Fork from here' creates a new thread whose messages are the history up to and including that message, with parentThreadID and forkedAtMessageID set. The new fork activates immediately. Pure Core logic for the fork computation (slice + metadata), UI affordance via message context menu. Fork lineage is data the read_thread tool can later expose.
acceptance_criteria: Forking from a mid-thread message creates+activates a new thread containing exactly the messages up to the fork point with parent metadata set; original thread untouched; fork computation is a pure tested Core function; forked threads appear in the thread list; works across app restart
close_reason: Fork from any conversational message: pure ChatThreadHelpers.fork (slice incl. target, fresh id, parentThreadID/forkedAtMessageID lineage, injected clock/id per house precedent), controller forkThread(at:) saves+activates, ChatBubble context menu (chatForkFromHere), switcher shows arrow.triangle.branch on forked threads via additive lenient ThreadSummary.hasParent. Tool chips deliberately not forkable. 6 Core + 2 integration fork tests; 12/12 ThreadStoreTests green on merged main; strict lint 0; arch OK. ff-merged as 225482e.

title: read_thread agent tool: lookup by id, keyword search, semantic search
description: Let the LLM reference older conversations. New Core AgentTool read_thread (mirror VaultSearchTool patterns) with modes: (1) by id — return a thread transcript (compact rendering, token-budget aware truncation); (2) by keywords — rank threads by term matches over titles+message text; (3) semantic — on-device embeddings via NaturalLanguage NLEmbedding sentence vectors (no API cost, local-first) computed per thread at save time and cached, cosine-ranked at query time. Also list mode returning recent thread ids+titles so the model can discover threads. Infra implements search over the ThreadStore; embedding computation+cache behind a protocol so tests can stub vectors. Wire into ChatSessionController tools. Compact transcript rendering + ranking + truncation are pure tested Core functions.
acceptance_criteria: Tool schema exposes id/keywords/semantic/list modes; by-id returns compact transcript respecting a token budget; keyword mode ranks deterministically (tested with fixtures); semantic mode ranks by cosine over stubbed vectors in tests and uses NLEmbedding live; embeddings cached and invalidated on thread update; tool wired into chat sessions; all offline-testable
close_reason: read_thread AgentTool shipped: get/search/semantic/list modes, active-thread exclusion, token-budgeted transcripts. Pure Core ThreadTranscriptRenderer (head+tail elision, reusable for summarize), ThreadKeywordRanker (deterministic tf), ThreadSemanticRanker (cosine top-k). NLThreadEmbedder (on-device NLEmbedding, Infra-only) + EmbeddingIndexingThreadStore decorator with updatedAt-invalidated sidecar cache; graceful keyword degradation. Wired into ChatSessionController tools. Validated on merged main: 124 integration + 20 eval + 373 Core tests, strict lint 0, arch OK. Merged as bbd104c.

title: Chat threads: persist, list, switch, new, clear
description: Chats are currently a single ephemeral transcript. Introduce Threads: every chat is a persisted thread; users can list threads, switch between them, start a new (empty) chat, and clear/delete. Data model (Core, Sendable): ChatThread { id UUID, title, createdAt, updatedAt, parentThreadID?, forkedAtMessageID? (reserved for forking issue), messages [ChatMessage] }. Persist one JSON file per thread under {vault}/.punkrecords/threads/{id}.json via an Infra actor behind a Core protocol (ThreadStore), mirroring repository patterns. Migrate the existing persisted transcript (ChatSessionController loadTranscript/persistTranscript path) into a thread on first run so no one loses their current chat. Title auto-derives from the first user message (pure, tested); manual rename optional. UI: chat panel gets a thread list (toolbar menu or popover), New Chat, and per-thread delete. ChatSessionController owns active-thread state; keep the view thin per CLAUDE.md discipline.
acceptance_criteria: Sending a message persists to the active thread; thread list shows titles sorted by updatedAt; New Chat creates+activates an empty thread; switching threads restores messages; delete works with confirmation; legacy transcript migrated; title derivation + sorting + migration decisions are pure tested Core functions; ThreadStore has integration tests via TempVaultFactory; ChatTurnReducer tests stay green
close_reason: Persisted chat threads shipped: ChatThread Core model (schemaVersion 1, fork metadata reserved, full MessageContext/ToolCallInfo codable round-trip per schema addendum), ThreadStore actor protocol + FileSystemThreadStore (JSON-per-thread at .punkrecords/threads, atomic writes, body-free summaries), legacy transcript auto-migrated once and retired, controller-owned activeThread with new/switch/delete, thread switcher UI with a11y ids preserved. Validated on merged main: 117 integration + 20 eval + 321-suite Core green, strict lint 0, arch OK. ChatTurnUITests updated but needs one interactive run.

title: Ollama model selection via dropdown populated from /api/tags
description: Selecting an Ollama model currently requires hand-typing the model name into a text field; names like 'llama3.2:latest' are finicky (tag suffixes, exact spelling) and typos fail silently at request time. Ollama's local server exposes GET {baseURL}/api/tags listing installed models. Populate a dropdown from that endpoint so users pick instead of type. Keep manual entry as a fallback when the server is unreachable (or for a model not yet pulled), preserve the existing UserDefaults key (ProviderRegistry.DefaultsKey — no persisted key rename), respect the user-configured Ollama base URL, and refresh the list when Settings opens / base URL changes. Parsing of the tags response must be a pure, unit-tested function per CLAUDE.md discipline; network call lives in Infra.
acceptance_criteria: Settings shows a dropdown of installed Ollama models fetched from /api/tags honoring the configured base URL; selection persists to the same UserDefaults key as before; unreachable server degrades gracefully to manual text entry with the stored value intact; tags-response parsing and model-list ordering are unit-tested; build + swiftlint --strict + arch check green.
close_reason: Settings Ollama model is now a Picker fed by GET {baseURL}/api/tags (OllamaModelCatalog pure parsing/options in Core, 11 tests; OllamaModelListClient transport seam in Infra, 3 tests; 3s timeout + 300ms URL-edit debounce). Stored value injected when absent from server list; graceful fallback to manual TextField with Retry when unreachable; Enter-manually escape hatch; same ollama.model UserDefaults key. Build + strict lint + arch check green. Picker visuals manual-only.

title: Session paths silently cap Anthropic output at 1024 tokens — truncates long answers and corrupts long tool calls
description:
Found by LiveMusicDSPEvals S4 (2026-07-04): the agent called create_note SIX times for a synthesis note and every call arrived WITHOUT its content field — the note body was truncated out of the tool-call JSON. Root cause: AnyLanguageModel's AnthropicLanguageModel sends max_tokens: options.maximumResponseTokens ?? 1024 (AnthropicLanguageModel.swift:514 in ALM 0.8.0), and both SessionAgentRunner and SessionTextCompleter defaulted options to a bare GenerationOptions() — so every Anthropic round was capped at 1024 output tokens. Consequences: (1) any chat answer beyond ~750 words silently truncates; (2) tool calls whose arguments exceed the cap lose fields mid-JSON — create_note with a long body errors with 'Missing or empty content' on every retry until the round cap; (3) NoteCompiler wiki articles truncate.

FIX (landed with this issue): SessionAgentRunner and SessionTextCompleter defaults are now GenerationOptions(maximumResponseTokens: 8192). Verified by rerunning LiveMusicDSPEvals S4.

FOLLOW-UP GUIDANCE for whoever picks this up next: (a) 8192 is a pragmatic default, not a per-provider tuned one — fold a providerDefaults.maximumResponseTokens into PUNK-yw1's centralized provider capabilities; (b) consider surfacing stop_reason=max_tokens from ALM as an AgentEvent warning so truncation is never silent again (may need an upstream ALM issue — it exposes no usage/stop metadata, same gap as PUNK-4bu); (c) an eval asserting a >1024-token answer survives intact would regression-guard this (add to PUNK-x0y grading upgrades).
acceptance_criteria: LiveMusicDSPEvals S4 passes: create_note receives full content, note lands on disk, wikilinks resolve >= 60%. Long chat answers no longer truncate at ~4KB. Follow-ups (provider-tuned defaults, stop_reason surfacing) either done or filed against PUNK-yw1/upstream.
close_reason: Fixed: SessionAgentRunner and SessionTextCompleter default GenerationOptions(maximumResponseTokens: 8192); ALM Anthropic backend was capping at 1024. Verified: S4 create_note now lands the full synthesis note (20/22 wikilinks resolve). Follow-ups noted in description: provider-tuned defaults belong in PUNK-yw1, stop_reason surfacing is an upstream ALM gap alongside PUNK-4bu. Commit 2320264.

title: Wire the eval flywheel: driver for the orphaned scenario sets + EvalResultStore persistence
description:
The flywheel machinery (PromptVariant, EvalResultStore at ~/.punkrecords/eval-results, VariantComparator, trend API) is fully built and unit-tested but has NO production data: the 21 diverseScenarios and the liveScenarios sets in EvalVaultFixtures.swift are defined yet never executed, and no suite persists EvalReports. Make the flywheel real.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- Add a driver suite (e.g. FlywheelRunEvals in Tests/PunkRecordsEvalTests) that iterates EvalVaultFixtures.diverseScenarios through EvalHarness.runLiveSession with the current PromptVariant, aggregates into an EvalReport, and saves via EvalResultStore — gated on PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1 (cost: 21 scenarios/run; consider PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_SAMPLE=n to run a random-n subset per invocation... but NOTE determinism: pick the subset by index hash of scenario id + date, not RNG, so reruns are comparable).
- Persisted reports unlock EvalResultStore.trend() — add a tiny report printer (scenario pass-rate, mean tool calls, token estimate) so a run ends with a human-readable delta vs the previous stored report.
- A/B path: EvalHarness.compareVariantsLiveSession exists and is uncalled — wire it behind an env flag (PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_VARIANT_B=terse-v2) to compare stored variants and emit a VariantComparator Recommendation.
- Grading upgrades to consider while in here (from the 2026-07-03 survey): apply hasWikilinks/tool-sequence checks the note-creation scenarios already support but never use; require a text-only final round (post PUNK-dpl the runner distinguishes narration) before counting requiredContent as satisfied.
- Cadence: document a suggested weekly run in CLAUDE.md; a scheduled agent can own this later.
acceptance_criteria: One live invocation runs the diverse set (or its deterministic sample), persists an EvalReport, and prints a trend delta against the prior stored report. A/B invocation produces a VariantComparison with a recommendation. Reports survive across runs in ~/.punkrecords/eval-results.
close_reason: FlywheelRunEvals driver added (gated on PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS, deterministic PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_SAMPLE via FNV-1a day-salted hash, A/B via PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_VARIANT_B wiring compareVariantsLiveSession). EvalReportPrinter trend summary + EvalResultStore persistence. 8 unit tests pass on merged main; gate-skip proven with zero API calls. Live run itself not executed (real cost) — first weekly run pending. Grading upgrades (hasWikilinks/tool-sequence) deliberately skipped: hasWikilinks grades finalOutput not note body, would misfire. Merged; validated at HEAD.

title: SessionAgentRunner ends the agent loop on narration text emitted alongside tool calls
description:
Found by the first real-vault run of LiveMusicDSPEvals (PUNK-89e, 2026-07-03): 4 of 6 scenarios terminated with the model's planning preamble recorded as the final answer, immediately after successful tool rounds. Examples: S2 ended with 'Let me grab the full VA filter paper before answering.' after 2 searches + 2 reads; S4 ('create a synthesis note') ended with 'I'll search the vault broadly... before writing the note.' and never called create_note; S6 likewise never captured. Transcripts archived with PUNK-89e notes.

ROOT CAUSE: SessionAgentRunner.run's round loop (Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/LLMProviders/SessionAgentRunner.swift, the 'if !text.isEmpty ... break' in the 0..<maxToolRounds loop) treats ANY non-empty response.content as the final answer. But AnyLanguageModel's session.respond executes the round's tool calls AND returns whatever prose the model emitted around them. Claude routinely narrates intent ('Let me read X first') while calling tools on multi-hop tasks, so the loop exits one round early and the narration ships as the answer. This hits the chat panel (same runner), not just evals — any multi-hop request can truncate.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
1. Detect whether the round actually ran tools: snapshot toolLog.count before session.respond, diff after (EventEmittingToolAdapter appends to toolLog on toolEnd). 
2. New termination rule: text + zero tools this round = final answer (break). Text + tools this round = narration: yield it as .textToken progress so the chat UI can show it, but CONTINUE the loop so the next round folds the new tool results. Empty text + tools = continue (unchanged). The forceAnswer final round stays as-is.
3. Consider folding the narration into the next roundPrompt (e.g. an 'Assistant plan so far' section) so the model doesn't re-plan from scratch — optional, evaluate.
4. Regression coverage: extend ScriptedLanguageModel so a scripted round can emit text AND call a tool in the same round (today .emitText unconditionally ends the run — mirror of the bug), then add a SessionContextThreadingEvals/TaskCompletionEvals case asserting the loop continues past a narrated tool round. Per CLAUDE.md discipline, the termination rule should be a pure, unit-testable decision function.
5. Verify live with: TEST_RUNNER_PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1 TEST_RUNNER_PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_VAULT=<vault-copy> xcodebuild ... -only-testing:PunkRecordsEvalTests/LiveMusicDSPEvals — S2 should mention TPT/zero-delay, S4/S6 should call create_note.
acceptance_criteria: Scripted regression test proves a text+tool round does not end the loop and a text-only round does. LiveMusicDSPEvals rerun: S2, S4, S6 no longer terminate on preamble text (S4/S6 call create_note). Chat panel still streams narration text as it arrives. No regression in SessionContextThreadingEvals.
close_reason: Fixed: roundOutcome pure rule — text ends the loop only when the round ran no tools; narration streams as progress and folds into the next round prompt. Regression tests in SessionAgentRunnerTests (pure rule) and SessionAgentRunnerToolEventTests (scripted narrated round). Live verification: LiveMusicDSPEvals went 1/6 honest passes to 6/6 (with PUNK-xu7). Commit 2320264.

title: Epic: Live Preview editing mode — Obsidian-style marker folding on TextKit 1
description:
Evolve the hybrid editor (PUNK-1hy) from dim-markers to hide-markers: markdown stays the source of truth, elements render styled, and syntax markers collapse to zero width when the caret is outside the element, revealing on entry — the Obsidian Live Preview model. Feasibility analysis (2026-07-03) concluded this is an increment, not a rewrite: heading scaling, styled emphasis, caret-proximity tracking, and pill chips already exist; the missing primitive is zero-width glyph folding via NSLayoutManagerDelegate NSGlyphProperty.null, which keeps source text unmutated (undo/find/FTS-safe).

PHASES (this epic depends on all four; close it when they are done or explicitly cut):
- PUNK-ef9 phase 0: fold primitive — pure fold-range computation + glyph folding + scoped invalidation
- PUNK-zp1 phase 1: fold inline markers (emphasis/code/headings/wikilink brackets), clickable markdown links, live-preview toggle
- PUNK-ueo phase 2: clickable checkboxes, drawn horizontal rules, blockquote bars, code-fence chrome
- PUNK-5ln phase 3: inline image previews via paragraph-spacing gap drawing (no text mutation)
PUNK-czt (heading folding) now also depends on phase 0 — same primitive, section-sized ranges.

INVARIANTS FOR ALL PHASES (implementation guardrails for coding agents):
1. Never mutate the source string for rendering. Decoration is attributes + glyph properties + layout-manager drawing only. The sole permitted text edits are explicit user actions (checkbox toggle) routed through shouldChangeText/didChangeText.
2. Stay on TextKit 1. The TK2 rejection in PUNK-1hy and PunkRecords_Wysiwyg_research.md stands; do not introduce NSTextLayoutManager.
3. Search/undo correctness: Cmd+F and FTS operate on the raw string and must keep matching folded text.
4. Perf: all passes scan only EditorDecorationRange.scanRange; fold changes invalidate only affected paragraphs.
5. IME: skip decoration while hasMarkedText() (existing guard in Coordinator.runDecorations).
6. Per CLAUDE.md discipline: pure functions + unit tests first, one XCUITest golden path, pure-visual checks manual and noted in close reasons.

NON-GOALS: WYSIWYG table editing, rendered note embeds (![[Note]]), math rendering, full Obsidian parity. If those become must-haves, the documented escape hatch is CodeMirror 6 in WKWebView (research doc Option C) — a rewrite decision, not part of this epic.
acceptance_criteria: All four phase issues closed or explicitly descoped with reasons. Live Preview on: markers fold/reveal per element; links/checkboxes/rules/quotes/images render as widgets. Live Preview off: editor identical to pre-epic behavior. No source-mutation regressions (undo, Cmd+F, FTS verified).

title: Live Preview phase 1: fold inline markers (emphasis, code, headings, wikilink brackets) + render markdown links
description:
First user-visible Live Preview slice — delivers most of the Obsidian feel. Depends on the PUNK-ef9 folding primitive.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
1. New decorator pass LivePreviewDecorator in Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/Editor/, applying .punkFolded ranges from MarkerFolding. Run it LAST in Coordinator.runDecorations (RawEditorView.swift) so it wins on overlapping ranges (current order: decorator, wikilinkDecorator).
2. Fold when caret is outside the element: ** __ bold, * _ italic, ~~ strikethrough, backticks around inline code, ATX heading hash prefixes including the trailing space, and the [[ ]] brackets of wikilinks. Styled rendering already exists (tree-sitter text.strong/text.emphasis fonts, HybridUXDecorator heading sizes), so hiding markers completes the effect. Keep the current dim-only behavior for source mode.
3. Markdown links: for [text](url) show only the styled text (theme.linkColor + underline), fold the brackets, parens, and url. Click opens the URL in the default browser. Mirror the existing closure pattern: PillTextView already has resolveWikilinkClick/resolveTagClick + onWikilinkClick/onTagClick — add resolveLinkClick with a pure hit-test function linkTarget(at:in:) modeled on WikilinkDecorator.wikilinkTarget, unit-tested (exclude image refs starting with an exclamation mark, exclude links inside code spans/fences).
4. Mode toggle: @AppStorage editor.livePreview (default true), surfaced in Settings and/or the editor toolbar next to the existing Preview button. When off, the editor behaves exactly as today — this is the escape hatch if folding misbehaves, and the comparison lever.
5. Expected UX: text reflows slightly when markers reveal on caret entry — Obsidian does the same; do not fight it, just keep invalidation scoped (phase 0).
6. XCUITest golden path in Tests/PunkRecordsUITests: type **bold**, move caret away, assert asterisks hidden; move caret inside, assert revealed. Follow ChatTurnUITests patterns; note UI tests need an interactive session with automation permission (CLAUDE.md Testing discipline).
acceptance_criteria: With caret outside: emphasis/strikethrough/inline-code delimiters, heading hashes, and wikilink brackets occupy zero width; caret entering the element reveals raw source. Markdown links render as styled clickable text that opens the browser; wikilink pill click-to-open still works. Source-mode toggle restores current dim-only behavior. Pure hit-test and fold-set functions unit-tested; one XCUITest covers the type-fold-reveal golden path.
close_reason: Merged to main (1a39d8a), validated by orchestrator: MarkerFolding extended with strikethrough, ATX heading hash prefixes (+exactly one trailing space; caret-on-line reveals; #tag excluded), wikilink brackets, markdown links (label styled linkColor+underline, [ ](url) tail folded, click opens browser via new pure linkTarget(at:in:) hit test excluding images/code/fences). Fenced-block exclusion (backtick+tilde, unterminated-aware, scanRange-scoped — documented limitation). Precedence: code > wikilink > link > strong > strike > emphasis. LivePreviewDecorator (renamed from MarkerFoldDecorator) keeps scoped paragraph-diff invalidation; editor.livePreview AppStorage toggle (default ON) in Settings Editor tab restores dim-only mode when off. 40 new Infra tests (290 green post-merge); byte-identical source + empty undo stack asserted across fold/unfold/disable. LivePreviewUITests WRITTEN + compile-checked but NOT executed (requires interactive automation session — testmanagerd wedge risk headless). Manual: zero-width rendering feel, pill shrinkage, heading indent shift, link-click, toggle live effect.

title: Live Preview phase 0: marker-fold primitive (fold-range computation + TextKit 1 glyph folding)
description:
Foundation for Obsidian-style Live Preview (feasibility analysis 2026-07-03; builds on epic PUNK-1hy, which deliberately chose TextKit 1 — do NOT migrate to TextKit 2 for this). Goal: a reusable mechanism that visually hides markdown marker characters (zero width) while the caret is outside their element, WITHOUT ever mutating the source string, so undo, find-over-raw-source, and FTS stay correct. This is the same primitive PUNK-czt (heading folding) should later reuse.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
1. Pure fold computation. New file Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/Editor/MarkerFolding.swift. API sketch: static func foldRanges(in text: NSString, scanRange: NSRange, caret: Int) -> [NSRange]. Compute PER-ELEMENT: caret anywhere inside the whole element (e.g. **bold** including both delimiters) reveals ALL markers of that element. The existing HybridUXDecorator marker regexes match delimiters independently and cannot pair them — either write pairing regexes with capture groups for both delimiter ranges, or derive element ranges from tree-sitter markdown_inline (see TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter.makeInlineConfiguration). Regex pairing is an acceptable first cut. All ranges are UTF-16 (NSString semantics).
2. Attribute + glyph folding. Add NSAttributedString.Key.punkFolded following the .pillBackground extension pattern at the top of WikilinkPillLayoutManager.swift. Implement NSLayoutManagerDelegate layoutManager(_:shouldGenerateGlyphs:properties:characterIndexes:font:forGlyphRange:): for each character index carrying .punkFolded, set NSGlyphProperty.null in the properties buffer, call setGlyphs(...), return glyphRange.length; return 0 when the range has no folded chars so default generation runs. Simplest wiring: WikilinkPillLayoutManager sets itself as its own delegate in init.
3. Scoped invalidation — THE perf-critical part. The decorator pass (invoked from Coordinator.runDecorations in RawEditorView.swift) must diff the new fold set against the previous one and call invalidateGlyphs(forCharacterRange:changeInLength:actualCharacterRange:) + invalidateLayout(forCharacterRange:actualCharacterRange:) ONLY for paragraphs whose fold state changed. Never invalidate the whole document on caret moves: existing passes are color-only (cheap display invalidation), but folding changes layout, so unscoped invalidation will visibly lag typing in large notes.
4. Respect existing guards: skip while textView.hasMarkedText() (IME composition — see Coordinator.runDecorations) and restrict scanning to EditorDecorationRange.scanRange(for:).
5. Housekeeping: run xcodegen generate after adding files; swiftlint before commit; build with -skipMacroValidation (CLAUDE.md).
acceptance_criteria: Fold-range computation is a pure function with unit tests in PunkRecordsInfraTests covering: delimiter pairing, caret inside/outside/at element boundaries, unbalanced or unterminated markers, and UTF-16 offsets with emoji/CJK content. Folded characters render at zero width while textView.string stays byte-identical to file content. Cmd+F finds text inside folded ranges. Undo stack is untouched by fold/unfold. Caret movement does not trigger full-document relayout (verify by instrumenting invalidation ranges).
close_reason: Merged to main (7424338), validated by orchestrator: MarkerFolding pure fold-range computation (delimiter pairing with precedence: code > strong > emphasis; caret-in-element reveal; UTF-16 semantics) + MarkerFoldDecorator scoped paragraph invalidation + WikilinkPillLayoutManager null-glyph delegate. 33 new unit tests; 250 Infra tests green post-merge; build + swiftlint --strict + check-architecture all pass. No string mutation anywhere (undo/find/FTS safe). Manual-only checks remaining (noted per CLAUDE.md discipline): on-screen zero-width rendering feel and Cmd+F visual verification. Known phase-0 caveats documented in MarkerFolding.swift: literal delimiter inside a span defeats regex pairing; intraword underscores fold.

title: Extract chat orchestration from LLMChatPanel
description: Design analysis finding: LLMChatPanel is a 636-line SwiftUI view that renders UI, stores transcript state, builds attachment prompts, resolves providers, builds ContextBuilder instructions, constructs AgentTools, streams SessionAgentRunner events, and reports issues. Principle: Ousterhout deep modules and Pragmatic Programmer orthogonality; the view should present a small interface over a deeper chat-turn/session coordinator. Evidence: Sources/PunkRecordsApp/Views/LLMChatPanel.swift lines around 329-577 mix send orchestration, provider factory calls, context building, tool construction, stream-to-message mapping, transcript persistence, and error handling. Suggested direction: introduce a @MainActor ChatSessionController/ChatTurnCoordinator with injected repository/search/model factory/transcript store. Keep LLMChatPanel focused on rendering, bindings, and commands.
acceptance_criteria: LLMChatPanel delegates send/load/persist/provider availability to a testable controller; chat-turn event mapping has unit tests independent of SwiftUI; existing ChatTurnUITests still pass through the real session path.
close_reason: ChatSessionController (@MainActor @Observable) extracted from LLMChatPanel (636→310 lines); AgentEvent→transcript folding is now pure ChatTurnReducer in Core with 11 unit tests incl. end-to-end over ScriptedLanguageModel through real SessionAgentRunner. ChatMessage/ToolCallInfo/MessageContext moved to Core as Sendable values. Validated on merged main: build, arch check, 99 integration + 11 reducer tests pass. ChatTurnUITests needs one interactive run (automation permission) to fully certify — identifiers preserved.

title: Fix search metadata filters and cover tag/title semantics
description: Design analysis finding: SearchQueryParser parses tag: and title: filters, but SQLiteSearchIndex only applies tag filtering and stores tag_json as base64-encoded JSON while querying it with LIKE against the raw tag text. That makes a public query feature easy to misunderstand and likely incorrect. Principle: Pragmatic Programmer design-by-contract and Ousterhout obscurity reduction; parsed query semantics should have one obvious implementation path. Evidence: Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/Search/SQLiteSearchIndex.swift parses titleFilter but never applies it, and tag_json storage/query encoding do not match. Suggested direction: store tags in a queryable representation or normalized table, apply titleFilter explicitly, and add integration tests that index documents then search with tag: and title:.
acceptance_criteria: Indexed search returns expected results for tag:<tag> and title:<title> filters, rejects/handles punctuation safely, and has tests at parser plus SQLiteSearchIndex level.
close_reason: Root cause: tags stored base64-JSON but queried with raw LIKE (tag: never matched); titleFilter parsed but never applied. Fixed: normalized document_tags table with exact bound-param matching, escaped case-insensitive title: substring match, metadata-only query support, schema v2 with user_version rebuild gate. 20 new tests; 47 tests in the search suites pass on merged main.

title: Add native PDF/document blocks to model session path

Decide whether to fork/patch AnyLanguageModel, add a PunkRecords-owned provider payload layer, or keep PDFs text-extracted only.

title: Heading outline parser (Core primitive)
description: Shared Core service that parses a markdown body into its heading structure: a flat, document-order list of headings each with level (1-6), title, the NSRange of the heading line, the NSRange of its section (heading line through the end of its subtree — everything up to the next heading of same-or-higher level), and its ancestor heading-path. Must be fence-aware (ignore '#' lines inside fenced code blocks). Pure and unit-tested; no AppKit. Unblocks refile (PUNK-7a2: needs section ranges + paths), heading folding (PUNK-czt: needs foldable ranges), and the inspector (PUNK-4bz: needs per-heading structure).
acceptance_criteria: Parses ATX headings with correct level/title/heading-range/section-range/path; section range spans the full subtree; '#' inside fenced code blocks is not treated as a heading; setext and edge cases handled or documented; unit-tested.
close_reason: HeadingOutline Core parser: flat document-order list of HeadingNode (level, title, headingRange, sectionRange spanning the full subtree, ancestor path), fence-aware (ignores # inside code fences), ATX-validated (requires space after #s, rejects >6 and indented). Pure/AppKit-free, 10 unit tests. Unblocks PUNK-7a2/czt/4bz. Tests: Core 146 green; lint clean.

title: Emacs motions: Meta word/sentence/paragraph navigation
description: Implement Emacs Meta-key motions on top of the dispatch layer: M-f/M-b (forward/backward word), M-d (kill word forward), M-DEL (backward-kill-word), M-a/M-e (sentence), M-{/M-} (paragraph), M-< / M-> (buffer start/end). macOS already provides the Control motions (C-a/C-e/C-f/C-b/C-n/C-p/C-k/C-d/C-t) natively, so this focuses on the Meta layer NSTextView doesn't ship. Word boundaries should match Emacs semantics reasonably.
acceptance_criteria: With Emacs mode on: M-f/M-b move by word, M-d/M-DEL kill word forward/back, M-a/M-e by sentence, M-</M-> jump to buffer ends; selections/marks update correctly.
close_reason: Meta motions implemented via pure Core EmacsMotion (caretDestination + killRange, 14 tests): M-f/M-b word, M-a/M-e sentence, M-{/M-} paragraph (blank-line delimited), M-</M-> buffer ends, M-d/M-DEL word kill (undoable delete; kill-ring integration lands in PUNK-2nn). Coordinator.performEmacsCommand applies them. Tests: Core 120 green; build + lint clean.

title: Emacs mark, region & kill-ring (C-space/C-w/M-w/C-y/M-y)
description: Implement the Emacs mark + kill-ring model: C-space sets the mark (activates region selection that extends with motion), C-w kills (cuts) the region, M-w copies the region, C-y yanks the most recent kill, M-y after a yank cycles the kill-ring (yank-pop), C-g cancels the mark/region. Maintain a kill-ring data structure; integrate with the system pasteboard for C-w/M-w/C-y so copy/paste interops with the OS. Word/line kills from the motions task should also push onto the kill-ring.
acceptance_criteria: With Emacs mode on: C-space sets mark and region grows with motion; C-w/M-w cut/copy region (and hit the system pasteboard); C-y yanks; M-y after C-y cycles prior kills; C-g cancels the region.
close_reason: Mark/region/kill-ring/yank implemented. Core: pure EmacsKillRing (newest-first, capacity eviction, yank-pop rotate; 7 tests). Coordinator: C-Space set-mark, motions extend region from mark, C-w kill / M-w copy region (→ kill-ring + system pasteboard), C-y yank, M-y yank-pop (tracked via lastYankRange + isPerformingEmacsEdit guard so user edits clear state but Emacs ops don't), C-g cancels mark/region, word kills (M-d/M-DEL) now feed the ring. Extracted Emacs dispatch into EditorCoordinator+Emacs.swift to keep the file/type within limits. Stateful AppKit wiring needs manual validation; kill-ring unit-tested. Tests: Core 127 green; build + lint clean (0 errors).

title: Emacs mode: persisted toggle + keybinding dispatch infrastructure
description: Foundation for Emacs mode. Add a persisted 'Emacs keybindings' setting (AppStorage/UserDefaults) and a menu toggle (until the Settings UI in PUNK-yf7 lands). Build a keybinding dispatch layer in the PillTextView/Coordinator that, when the mode is on, intercepts Control/Meta chords in keyDown/doCommand(by:) and routes them to Emacs command handlers; when off, behavior is unchanged. Must not disrupt IME/marked-text composition. This task wires the plumbing + the toggle; specific commands land in the sibling tasks.
acceptance_criteria: A persisted Emacs-mode toggle (menu item) exists; with it on, a dispatch layer receives C-/M- chords in the editor and can consume or pass them through; with it off, no behavior change; marked-text/IME composition unaffected.
close_reason: Emacs dispatch infrastructure + toggle. Core: EmacsCommand vocabulary, EmacsKeyChord, pure EmacsKeymap (dictionary lookup, 6 tests). App: NSEvent→chord decoder, PillTextView.keyDown interception (skips IME + active completion popover), Coordinator.performEmacsCommand dispatch (implements keyboardQuit/C-g; recognized-but-unimplemented commands consumed so Meta chords don't insert garbage), @AppStorage('editor.emacsKeybindings') persisted toggle + Editor menu item, threaded through the representable. Extracted PillTextView to its own file. Tests: Core 106 green; build clean; lint clean.

title: Preview: clickable links — external URLs, [[wikilinks]], #tags
description: Make links work in the MarkdownUI preview. Standard [text](url) should open external URLs in the browser. Preprocess the rendered body to convert [[wikilinks]] and #tags into markdown links with a custom scheme (punk://note/<title>, punk://tag/<name>), then handle them via an OpenURLAction: note links select the note (or offer create like the editor's click-to-create), tag links set the sidebar filter to tag:<name>, everything else opens externally. Reuse WikilinkDecorator regexes / AppState.createNote / sidebarFilterQuery.
acceptance_criteria: In preview: external links open in the default browser; [[wikilinks]] navigate to the note (or prompt to create when missing); #tags set the sidebar filter; alias links [[Target|alias]] resolve to Target.
close_reason: Preview links now work. New Core PreviewLinkRewriter converts [[wikilinks]] and #tags to punk://note/ and punk://tag/ markdown links, skipping fenced code blocks and inline code spans (so #include/[[x]] in samples aren't mangled). MarkdownPreviewView routes punk:// via OpenURLAction (note→open/create, tag→sidebar filter) and lets everything else open externally. Tests: Core 100 green (+9 rewriter).

title: Preview: tree-sitter syntax highlighting in fenced code blocks
description: Implement a MarkdownUI CodeSyntaxHighlighter that statically highlights each code block. MarkdownUI hands us (code, language); resolve the language via the shared TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter.makeLanguageProvider(), parse the code with SwiftTreeSitter (Parser->Tree), run the config's .highlights Query, collect captures (range+name), and build an AttributedString applying the same color mapping (attributes(for:theme:)) the editor uses. Falls back to plain monospaced text for unknown/unsupported languages. No markdown injection needed (MarkdownUI already splits code blocks).
acceptance_criteria: Code fences in the preview pane colorize for every editor-supported language (swift/python/ts/js/rust/c/cpp/bash/json); unknown languages render as plain mono text; colors match the editor's Dracula palette.
close_reason: MarkdownUI CodeSyntaxHighlighter (TreeSitterCodeSyntaxHighlighter) backed by a new static span extractor TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter.codeHighlightSpans (parse + highlights query + predicate resolution). Wired into MarkdownPreviewView; shares the editor's grammar/Dracula palette. Pure static helpers marked nonisolated so the preview path runs off the live @MainActor highlighter. Tests: Infra 115 green (+4 static-highlight).

title: Epic: Editor navigation + full Emacs keybinding mode
description: Add a toggleable, full Emacs keybinding mode to the NSTextView-based editor (user is an Emacs user). macOS NSTextView already provides C-a/C-e/C-f/C-b/C-n/C-p/C-k/C-d/C-t and basic C-y; Emacs mode layers on the Meta motions, an explicit mark + kill-ring, yank/yank-pop, and a handful of editing commands. Gated behind a persisted setting so non-Emacs users are unaffected.
acceptance_criteria: A persisted 'Emacs mode' toggle exists; when on, Meta word-motions, set-mark/region, kill-ring (C-w/M-w/C-y/M-y), and core editing chords work in the editor; when off, default macOS behavior is unchanged; IME/marked-text composition is not disrupted.
close_reason: All children complete: PUNK-9y5 (toggle + dispatch infra), PUNK-0cy (Meta motions), PUNK-2nn (mark/region/kill-ring/yank), PUNK-9ls (undo/save/case/transpose). Full Emacs keybinding mode behind a persisted toggle; key→command mapping and all text math are pure/unit-tested in Core, AppKit wiring is a thin shell. Stateful interaction (mark/region/yank, C-x prefix) needs manual in-app validation.

title: Epic: Preview rendering polish (syntax highlighting, working links, theme)
description: The read-only MarkdownUI preview pane (MarkdownPreviewView, also used for chat messages) currently renders with the stock .gitHub theme: code blocks are unstyled, links are not wired to navigation, and the palette doesn't match the editor. Bring preview to parity with the editor: tree-sitter code highlighting, clickable links (external + internal wikilinks/tags), and a theme consistent with the editor (Dracula/dark-mode aware). Reuses the language provider + color mapping from TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter.
acceptance_criteria: Code fences in preview are syntax-highlighted for all editor-supported languages; standard [text](url) links open externally; [[wikilinks]] navigate (or offer create) and #tags filter the sidebar; preview palette matches the editor theme in light and dark mode.
close_reason: All children complete: PUNK-3wk (code syntax highlighting), PUNK-2a5 (clickable links), PUNK-ku1 (theme alignment). Preview now matches the editor: tree-sitter code highlighting, working wikilink/tag/external links, and an EditorTheme-derived MarkdownUI theme.

title: Editor body text illegible in dark mode
description: Reported with screenshot: in dark mode, all body text (paragraphs, list items, status lines) rendered dim/illegible while only headings stayed bright. Root cause: Neon's TextStorageSystemInterface resets each styled range to textView.typingAttributes before applying token colors; the editor never set an explicit adaptive foreground in typingAttributes, so untokenized body text inherited a fallback that's illegible on a dark background. Heading text was fine only because tree-sitter applies an explicit .textColor to the text.title token.
close_reason: Fixed: editor now sets explicit typingAttributes ({mono font, .foregroundColor: .textColor}) and seeds content via setAttributedString with the same base attrs, so Neon's reset-to-typingAttributes uses an adaptive foreground. Added HybridUXDecorator.fillBodyColor as a safety net that gives any range left without a foreground an adaptive .textColor (without overriding tree-sitter token colors). 2 new regression tests; all 91 Infra tests pass.

title: Editor W4: Wikilink pill chips + tag chips + in-editor autocomplete
description:
PKM-specific syntax. The hardest piece of the build — budget the full week.

Wikilinks:
- Render `[[Note]]` and `[[Note|alias]]` as a pill chip via NSTextAttachment with a custom NSTextAttachmentCellProtocol cell. Cell draws a rounded background, the link text, and (if alias present) shows the alias.
- Caret-inside-range: collapse the chip and reveal raw `[[ ]]` source. Caret-outside-range: show chip, hide brackets via zero-width kerning or attachment replacement.
- Click on chip opens the target note (calls into the existing document repository).
- Unresolved links (target file does not exist) render with a distinct style (e.g., dashed underline) and click-to-create.

Autocomplete:
- `[[` triggers a fuzzy-match popover anchored to the caret. Source: existing FTS5 title index (or trigram index — whichever the SQLiteSearchIndex actor supplies).
- ↑/↓/Enter to select; Esc dismisses.
- Continue to update results as the user types after `[[`.

Tags:
- `#tag` and `#nested/tag` render as a lower-contrast colored pill (color derived from hash for stability).
- Autocomplete on `#` once the third character is typed; backed by a tags table maintained by the background indexer.

This sub-issue SUPERSEDES the in-editor autocomplete half of PUNK-913. The backlinks panel + link graph table portion of PUNK-913 remains as a separate side-panel feature.
acceptance_criteria:
- [[Existing Note]] renders as a pill chip when caret is outside; reverts to `[[Existing Note]]` source when caret enters
- Click on chip opens the target note in the editor
- [[Nonexistent]] renders with the unresolved style; click prompts to create
- Typing `[[fo` shows an autocomplete popover with fuzzy matches; Enter inserts `[[Foo Note]]`
- #tag renders as a colored pill; click filters notes by tag (or opens a tag view, deferred to a separate issue)
- Autocomplete latency < 50ms p99 on a 10k-note vault
close_reason: Visual + click-to-open core delivered (per scoping decision): WikilinkPillLayoutManager draws rounded pill backgrounds (TextKit 1) for [[wikilinks]] and #tags; WikilinkDecorator applies pills when caret is outside and reveals raw source when caret enters; resolved links use accent color, unresolved get a dashed underline; #tags get stable hash-derived colors. Click on a resolved pill opens the target note (PillTextView hit-testing). Editor rebuilt on an explicit TK1 stack to host the custom layout manager. 15 new tests pass. Deferred to follow-ups: [[ autocomplete (PUNK-06f), click-to-create for unresolved (PUNK-1wc), tag autocomplete + click-to-filter (PUNK-y4d).

title: Editor W5: ⌘F find/replace (NSTextFinder) + ⌘O quick-open
description:
Editor-level search affordances. Uses TextKit 1's built-in NSTextFinder for free.

Find/Replace (⌘F, ⌘⌥F):
- Wire NSTextFinder against the editor's NSTextView. Free find bar, regex toggle, case-insensitive option, find-all-highlight.
- CRITICAL: search must operate over `textStorage.string` (raw source), NOT the displayed/decorated runs. This fixes the "Bear bug" where search misses content inside dimmed/hidden markers (e.g., searching `foo` in `**foo**` when the `**` is hidden).
- Replace operates on the underlying string; decorations recompute via the usual textStorage delegate path.

Quick Open (⌘O):
- Modal sheet, single text field, fuzzy match against vault titles via the existing SQLiteSearchIndex (FTS5).
- Looks/feels like Xcode's "Open Quickly".
- Enter opens the file in the active editor pane.

Explicitly out of scope: migrating SQLiteSearchIndex from raw SQLite to GRDB.swift (the research doc suggests this — defer; it's a separate decision the codebase already has a working answer for).
acceptance_criteria:
- ⌘F opens the find bar; finds matches inside dimmed/hidden markdown markers
- Regex search works via NSTextFinder's built-in toggle
- ⌘⌥F opens replace UI; replacements update the textStorage and trigger normal re-decoration
- ⌘O opens fuzzy quick-open over vault titles; ↑/↓/Enter navigation
- All flows respect VoiceOver
close_reason: Delivered: (1) ⌘O Quick Open palette wired through new QuickOpenView + QuickOpenMatcher (Core) — fuzzy subsequence match with word-boundary and consecutive-run bonuses, ↑/↓/Enter navigation, Esc dismisses. (2) ⌘F find/replace handled by NSTextView's standard NSTextFinder (already had usesFindBar=true + isIncrementalSearchingEnabled=true since W1). Moved 'Find by Content' from ⌘⌥F to ⌘⌥⇧F so ⌘⌥F is free for the standard Edit > Find and Replace menu. 10 matcher tests + UI test pass. Search operates on textStorage.string so dimmed markers are still searchable (fixes the 'Bear bug').

title: Editor W2: Parser pipeline — swift-markdown + Neon + tree-sitter-markdown
description:
Replace W1's regex stub with the real parsing pipeline.

Two parsers, two purposes:
1. swift-markdown (Apple, cmark-gfm under the hood) — canonical AST. Used on save/open/export. Correctness path; ~1ms parse for a 50KB note is fine.
2. Neon + SwiftTreeSitter + tree-sitter-markdown — live in-editor styling. Incremental, low-latency. Tree-sitter is fast+approximate for Markdown; pair with swift-markdown for canonical decisions.

Bridge implementation:
- On `textStorage(_:didProcessEditing:range:changeInLength:)`, feed the edit delta to `TreeSitterClient.didChangeContent(in:delta:)`
- Receive an invalidation RangeSet; reapply attributes only to those ranges (do NOT rehighlight the whole document)
- Token → attribute table: heading, emphasis, strong, code_span, fenced_code, link, image, list_item, etc. all map to NSAttributedString.Key entries
- Pass plain `String` + `[HighlightRange]` across actor boundaries — never NSAttributedString (not Sendable)

Libraries: github.com/swiftlang/swift-markdown, github.com/ChimeHQ/Neon, github.com/tree-sitter/tree-sitter-markdown grammar.
acceptance_criteria:
- Incremental highlight latency p99 < 50ms on the edited paragraph of a 100KB note
- swift-markdown canonical parse runs on save (and optionally on open) and feeds future export/Quick Look paths
- Highlight survives all CommonMark + GFM constructs in a fixture corpus (headings, lists, tables, links, images, fences, blockquotes, hr)
- W1's regex pipeline is removed; no double-styling races
close_reason: Delivered: swift-markdown + Neon + tree-sitter-markdown wired in. New TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter (Infra) wraps Neon.TextViewHighlighter for incremental live styling. New CanonicalMarkdownParser (Core) runs on save via swift-markdown. RegexSyntaxHighlighter + protocol removed. 20 new tests pass (7 canonical-parser + 13 highlighter). Editor preview UI test passes. Wikilink/tag styling intentionally deferred to PUNK-6du (W4).

title: Editor W3: Hybrid UX — marker dimming, heading sizes, code blocks, link styling
description:
The "Bear / iA Writer feel" layer. Markers stay visible but visually recede; caret proximity reveals them at full contrast.

Deliverables:
- Caret-proximity marker dimming: for each token whose range is more than ~2 chars from the caret, set foregroundColor to NSColor.tertiaryLabelColor; within proximity, NSColor.labelColor. Recompute on every selection change.
- Heading font scaling: H1 ~1.7x, H2 ~1.4x, H3 ~1.2x, H4–H6 baseline + bold. Same family as body for visual coherence.
- Bold/italic glyph weights: synthesize via NSFontManager when the user's editor font lacks a true italic/bold cut
- Fenced code blocks: monospaced font + subtle background fill (paragraph style with .backgroundColor). Inline token coloring via Splash or Highlightr keyed on the fence's language tag
- Inline code: monospaced + background tint
- Links: underline + accent color; blockquote: left border + indent + dimmer foreground; horizontal rules: full-width thin line via paragraph spacing

Decoration model: keep a `RangeSet` of decoration ranges and recompute diffs on selection change. Reapply attributes only to the diff (mirrors CodeMirror 6's Decoration model in pattern).
acceptance_criteria:
- Markers visibly dim when caret moves away, reveal when caret approaches (≤2 chars)
- Headings render at appropriate scaled sizes
- Fenced ```swift / ```python / ```js blocks have syntax-colored content
- Code/quote/HR all visually distinct from body text
- Decoration recompute on selection change completes within 16ms (one frame)
close_reason: Delivered: HybridUXDecorator (Infra) overlays per-heading font scaling, caret-proximity marker dimming (≤2 chars reveals), blockquote color, and horizontal-rule color on top of TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter. Wired through RawEditorView.Coordinator on text/selection changes. Code-block backgrounds shipped in W2. 12 new tests pass — perf test confirms decoration on 500-line doc completes in well under one frame (16ms budget). Code-fence CONTENT syntax coloring (the per-language injection piece) carved out into PUNK-tlb so the decorator could ship clean.

title: Editor W1: NSTextView wrapper + basic shell (load/save, regex highlight, frontmatter dim)
description:
Foundation week. Goal: a working editable .md surface inside SwiftUI with stub styling.

Deliverables:
- `MarkdownTextView: NSViewRepresentable` wrapping `NSTextView.scrollableTextView()`
- `NSTextView.isRichText = false` — we own all attributes
- NSTextStorage delegate hook (`textStorage(_:didProcessEditing:range:changeInLength:)`)
- File I/O: load a `.md` from disk into an `NSAttributedString`, save back to disk preserving line endings + trailing newline; coordinator handles change tracking and dirty state
- Stub regex-based highlighting for `#` headings (font size by `#` count), `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `` `code` `` — debounced 50ms, full document; this is the fallback path and is replaced by Neon in W2
- Frontmatter detection: YAML block between `---` lines rendered with dimmed foreground color + monospaced font
- Swift 6 strict concurrency: NSTextView and its delegate are @MainActor; future parsing actor lives off-main

Explicitly out of scope: tree-sitter, swift-markdown integration, marker dimming based on caret proximity, pill chips, autocomplete.
acceptance_criteria:
- Open + edit + save round-trips a .md file with no content loss (whitespace, CRLF, trailing newline preserved)
- Headings, bold, italic, inline code visibly highlighted within 100ms of opening a 50KB note
- Frontmatter block dimmed when present
- Undo (⌘Z) and redo (⌘⇧Z) work via NSTextView's built-in UndoManager
- VoiceOver reads document content correctly
close_reason: Already implemented. Sources/PunkRecordsApp/Views/RawEditorView.swift:154 defines EditorTextViewRepresentable: NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSTextView.scrollableTextView() with a Coordinator on NSTextViewDelegate. Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/Editor/RegexSyntaxHighlighter.swift provides the regex-based highlighter. Load/save round-trip and dirty-state tracking happen via DocumentEditorViewModel. The editor epic effectively starts at PUNK-qpz (W2 — parser pipeline with swift-markdown + Neon).

title: Epic: Markdown editor — NSTextView + TextKit 1 + swift-markdown + Neon (Option B)
description:
Build the core editor surface for PunkRecords using TextKit 1, wrapped in SwiftUI via NSViewRepresentable. Hybrid-markdown UX (markers visible, dimmed near caret; wikilinks as pill chips when caret is outside; live-typed styling) modeled on Bear (pre-Panda) / iA Writer / Drafts.

Key decisions (see PunkRecords_Wysiwyg_research.md):
- TextKit 1 over TextKit 2. Evidence: Krzyżanowski's 4-year retrospective (7 unresolved FB radars); AltStore's public regret; MarkEdit explicitly rejecting TK2 for CodeMirror; Apple's own TextEdit falls back to TK1 for tables/printing per WWDC22 #10090.
- swift-markdown (cmark-gfm) for canonical parse on save/open/export — correctness path.
- Neon + SwiftTreeSitter + tree-sitter-markdown for live in-editor styling — incremental, low-latency path.
- MarkdownUI used ONLY for read-only preview panes (chat assistant messages, Quick Look), never the editor.
- NOT pursuing Option A (SwiftUI TextEditor) — pre-macOS 26 it cannot do pill chips / replaced ranges; revisit for v2.
- NOT pursuing Option C (WKWebView + CodeMirror 6) — accepts non-native feel + permanent JS/Swift maintenance split. CM6 is the strongest fallback only if Option B blows up.
- NOT pursuing Option D (native block editor) — wrong cost class for solo dev, wrong model for flat-file vault.

Unblocks: PUNK-czt (folding), PUNK-4bz (Inspector ⌘I integration), PUNK-ubz (DataDetectors), Week 4 of this epic supersedes the autocomplete half of PUNK-913 (backlinks panel remains in PUNK-913).

Fallback plan (documented in research §7) if Weeks 1–2 slip: regex-only highlighting on NSTextView, swift-markdown only at save time, no marker hiding, no pill chips — ~1500–2500 LOC, ships in 2 weeks. Tracked here as a contingency, not a separate issue.
acceptance_criteria:
- A vault .md file can be opened, edited, and saved through the new editor
- Hybrid styling: bold/italic/headings render in-place with markers visible but dimmed when caret is far
- [[wikilinks]] appear as pill chips when caret is outside; revert to source when caret enters
- ⌘F find/replace operates over raw source (catches matches inside dimmed markers — fixes the "Bear bug")
- 10MB single file remains responsive (incremental highlighting p99 < 50ms on edited paragraph)
- VoiceOver navigation works on headings and links (NSTextView accessibility free-tier)
close_reason: Epic delivered on main: TextKit 1 NSTextView editor wrapped via NSViewRepresentable (RawEditorView/EditorTextViewRepresentable/EditorTextKitFactory), hybrid marker styling near caret (HybridUXDecorator), tree-sitter incremental highlighting (TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter + Neon), wikilink pill chips with caret-aware reveal (WikilinkPillLayoutManager), swift-markdown canonical parse, MarkdownUI restricted to read-only preview. Open/edit/save works through the editor; find operates over raw source since decorations never mutate the string. Remaining verification carried by dedicated issues: accessibility/IME pass PUNK-cmd, 10MB perf criterion PUNK-flu. Live Preview marker folding continues in epic PUNK-efw.

title: Image attachment handler (HEIC normalize, resize, provider routing)
description:
Handler for user-attached images.

Accepted formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, HEIC. HEIC normalized to JPEG before sending (broadest provider compatibility).

Resize policy:
- Anthropic: max edge 1568px per [vision docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/vision); resize larger images preserving aspect ratio; never upscale
- OpenAI: provider auto-handles, but resize anything > 2048px max-edge to save tokens
- Apple Foundation Models: no image input — surface "this provider doesn't support images; switch to Claude or GPT?" with a switch-provider button

Chip metadata: thumbnail (40px), dimensions (e.g. "1024 × 768"), file size in KB/MB.
