Provider payload: image content block with base64 data + media type. Capability flag on LLMProvider: `nativeImageInput: Bool`.

Multi-image: each image is its own attachment chip; ordering preserved in the request.

Out of scope for v1: animated GIF handling (send first frame), image OCR pre-pass, screenshot annotation.
acceptance_criteria:
- PNG, JPEG, HEIC all attach with thumbnail + dimensions in chip
- HEIC converted to JPEG before send (verify base64 payload media type)
- Image >1568px max-edge resized for Anthropic provider
- Switching to Apple Foundation Models with images in composer surfaces blocking dialog with switch-provider CTA
- Multi-image conversation sends ordered image blocks in request
close_reason: Implemented ImageChatAttachmentHandler and app routing: image metadata now records dimensions and 40px thumbnail data for chips; HEIC/HEIF extension payloads normalize to JPEG; Anthropic images resize to 1568 max edge while OpenAI/Ollama use 2048; SessionAgentRunner can carry ordered native image segments through the existing tool loop; Apple/Foundation Models image sends show a switch-to-Claude/GPT dialog. Validated with ImageChatAttachmentHandlerTests, SessionAgentRunnerTests, SwiftLint on touched files, Xcode build, ChatTurnUITests/testSendMessageDrivesToolCallAndScriptedResponse, and ChatPanelUITests/testAttachmentControlsVisibleInComposer.

title: PDF attachment handler (provider-native + PDFKit fallback)
description:
Handler that routes a user-attached PDF to the right path based on provider capability.

Provider routing:
- Anthropic: send as `document` content block per [their PDF support](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/pdf-support) — up to 32MB and 100 pages
- OpenAI Responses API: send as `input_file` with file_data field
- Apple Foundation Models (text-only): extract text via PDFKit (`PDFDocument(url:).string`) and send as text block
- Capability detection: introduce `LLMCapabilities.nativePDFInput: Bool` on each provider; orchestrator picks path

Metadata for the chip:
- Page count (PDFKit can give this fast)
- File size
- Encrypted/locked detection — prompt for password, or refuse with clear message if blank-password unlock fails

Size policy:
- Hard cap at 32MB (Anthropic limit; OpenAI is generous; PDFKit fine with anything)
- Warn over 100 pages

Extracted-text path: preserve heading order by walking PDFKit's page-by-page text; do not attempt OCR for scanned PDFs in v1 — surface "this PDF appears to be scanned (no text extractable); image-OCR is not yet supported."
acceptance_criteria:
- Anthropic provider: PDF goes through as document content block (verifiable in wire trace)
- OpenAI provider: PDF goes through as input_file (verifiable in wire trace)
- Apple Foundation Models: PDF text-extracted; chip shows "(text extracted, N pages)"
- 32MB / 100-page warnings fire at correct thresholds
- Encrypted PDF surfaces a password prompt; cancelling cancels the attachment
- Scanned (no-text) PDF surfaces an explicit "no text extractable" error rather than sending empty content
notes: Implemented the PDFKit fallback path: PDF attachments are validated at attach time, page count/token metadata is added for chips, blank/scanned PDFs fail with a clear no-text error, >100 pages warns, >32MB hard-rejects, and send-time prompt construction appends extracted PDF text. Provider-native Anthropic/OpenAI PDF blocks are blocked by PUNK-pch because the current AnyLanguageModel session abstraction exposes text/structure/image segments but no PDF/document/file segment or OpenAI input_file path.

title: Chat composer attachment UX (chips, drag-drop, file picker, ⌘⇧A)
description:
SwiftUI composer extension for the chat surface.

Functionality:
- Drop zone: `.onDrop(of: [.fileURL, .image, .pdf])` on the composer container; visible overlay when a drag is in progress
- File picker: ⌘⇧A and a toolbar paperclip button open NSOpenPanel via `.fileImporter`; allowedContentTypes derived from registered handlers; allowsMultipleSelection true
- Attachment chips: HStack above the text field; each chip shows SF Symbol for type, filename (truncating middle), byte size, remove ✕
- Token estimate: live label in the composer footer ("~12.4k tokens"); updates as attachments and text change; uses existing TokenEstimator plus provider-specific image/PDF cost adapters
- Confirmation: if total estimated tokens > 50k OR any single attachment > 20MB, show NSAlert before send
- Persistence: each accepted attachment generates a security-scoped bookmark stored alongside the message in the chat transcript file (`<!-- attachment: {bookmark-base64, filename, size, type} -->` adjacent to the message text)

Does NOT do file content reading or provider payload building — those live in the per-type handler sub-issues. This issue is purely the input surface + state management.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dragging a file over the composer shows a visible drop affordance
- Dropping one or more files adds chips with correct icon, filename, and size
- ⌘⇧A opens NSOpenPanel scoped to registered content types
- Removing a chip removes the attachment from the pending message state
- Token estimate updates within 100ms of attachment change
- Confirmation NSAlert appears for >50k tokens or >20MB single file
- Security-scoped bookmark stored with the message; resolvable on reopen
notes: Implemented composer-side attachment UX foundation: paperclip file importer (Cmd-Shift-A), URL drop destination with drag affordance, pending attachment chips with remove action, attachment metadata/security-scoped bookmark creation, live token estimate, large-send confirmation policy, and user-message attachment rendering. Added Core policy tests and a UI smoke test for composer controls. Not closing yet: existing chat surface still lacks durable on-disk transcript persistence/reopen plumbing, so the bookmark metadata is stored on ChatMessage but not yet restored from disk. Filed PUNK-1fo for Keychain dialogs during UI test startup.
close_reason: Implemented attachment composer UX plus transcript persistence/reopen: paperclip/Cmd-Shift-A importer, URL drop target, removable chips, token estimate, large-send confirmation, security-scoped bookmark transcript comments, and chat transcript reload. Validated with Xcode build, ChatTurnUITests/testSendMessageDrivesToolCallAndScriptedResponse, ChatPanelUITests/testAttachmentControlsVisibleInComposer, and ChatAttachmentPolicyTests.

title: Text/code/markdown attachment handler
description:
Handler that converts a user-attached text/code/markdown file into a text block on the outgoing LLM request.

Responsibilities:
- Accept UTF-8 file contents from a security-scoped URL
- Detect binary content (NULL bytes in the first 8KB, or failed UTF-8 decode) and reject with a clear error
- Wrap content in a fenced block with a heading: filename + relative path from user's home dir (or absolute if outside ~)
- Surface byte count and estimated tokens back to the composer for the chip display
- File size policy: warn at 1MB, hard-cap at 10MB (configurable)

File types in v1: any UTF-8 text — .md, .txt, .swift, .py, .js, .ts, .json, .yaml, .toml, .html, .css, .sh, .rb, .go, .rs, .c, .cpp, .h, .java, .kt, .sql, .csv, .log, .xml. Driven by UTI conformance to `public.text` plus a small allowlist of source-code UTIs that don't inherit from public.text.

Not in scope here: PDF, image, .docx/.pages/.rtf. Those are separate handlers.
acceptance_criteria:
- Plain .txt + a Swift source file + a YAML config all attach as text blocks with correct filename headings
- Binary detection rejects a .o or .png attempted as text with a clear error message
- 1MB file warns; 11MB file is hard-rejected with explanation
- Non-UTF-8 (latin1) file rejected with "encoding not supported" rather than mojibake
- Estimated tokens reported back to composer match TokenEstimator(content)
close_reason: Implemented TextChatAttachmentHandler for UTF-8 text/code/markdown attachments: security-scoped reads, NULL-byte and UTF-8 validation, fenced prompt blocks with relative headings, 1MB warnings, 10MB hard cap, and content-based token estimates wired into the composer/send path. Validated with full PunkRecordsCore swift test suite, SwiftLint on touched files, Xcode build, ChatTurnUITests/testSendMessageDrivesToolCallAndScriptedResponse, and ChatPanelUITests/testAttachmentControlsVisibleInComposer.

title: Epic: File attachments in chat composer (read-only)
description:
Let users attach external files to a chat turn via drag-drop into the composer or NSOpenPanel ("Attach…" button / ⌘⇧A). Attachments are strictly read-only — PunkRecords never writes to the source location. The vault remains the only write surface.

Three attachment types, each provider-aware:
- Text / markdown / code → load as UTF-8 string, send as text block with filename + relative-path heading
- PDF → if provider advertises native PDF input (Anthropic, OpenAI), send as document block; otherwise extract text via PDFKit and send as text
- Image (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, HEIC) → resize to provider limits, normalize HEIC→JPEG, send as image block; on text-only providers (Apple Foundation Models today), surface an explicit "this provider doesn't support images — switch?" instead of silently dropping.

Composer UX:
- Drag overlay appears when files are dragged over the composer (accepts .fileURL, .image, .pdf)
- Multi-file attach in one drop / one picker invocation
- Each attachment shows as a removable chip above the text input (filename, type icon, byte size, ✕)
- Live token-estimate updates as attachments add/remove
- Confirmation prompt before send if estimated tokens > ~50k

Persistence:
- Attachments travel with the chat message in its on-disk transcript
- Reference by absolute path + security-scoped bookmark (so re-opens survive macOS sandbox reset and minor user moves)
- If a source file is missing on re-render, show "[file unavailable]" placeholder; do not auto-snapshot bytes into the transcript by default
- Future opt-in: "snapshot" mode that copies into `.punkrecords/attachments/{conversation-id}/` for archival

Provider integration notes:
- LLMOrchestrator + each LLMProvider gains a Capabilities surface declaring nativeImageInput / nativePDFInput / maxAttachmentBytes
- LLMRequest message blocks expand from text-only to (text | image | document)
- Existing AgentTool stack is unaffected — agent self-driven reads still go through ReadDocumentTool; user-driven attachments are the new path

Why: the chat surface currently sees only vault content via tools. Users want to bring outside material in — "read this paper", "look at this screenshot", "summarize this transcript" — without first copying it into the vault. Read-only attachment respects the user's filesystem while letting the agent reach anywhere.

Explicit scope cuts:
- No audio/video transcription (separate feature; Granola-style)
- No .docx/.pages/.rtf/.epub conversion (users can paste content)
- No server-side caching of attachment bytes — each turn re-reads from disk
- No write-back to source files (the user's emphasis; verifiable invariant)

Tracked via sub-issues for composer UX, text handler, PDF handler, image handler.
acceptance_criteria:
- Drag a mix of .md + .pdf + .png onto the composer → three chips appear → token estimate updates → send → LLM responds with awareness of all three
- ⌘⇧A and "Attach…" menu item open NSOpenPanel with relevant content types
- Files NOT written outside the vault under any circumstance — verified by filesystem audit test (no FileHandle for writing opened against any path outside the vault root during attachment flow)
- Reopening a chat with attachments resolves files via security-scoped bookmark; missing files show a clear "[file unavailable]" placeholder
- Switching provider mid-conversation surfaces a clear error if an existing attachment type is unsupported by the new provider

title: Refile picker (⌘⇧M) — fuzzy move heading to (file, heading-path)
description:
Modal sheet, single text field. Caret on a heading + ⌘⇧M → fuzzy-matched results showing `File ▸ Heading ▸ Sub-heading` paths. Enter moves the heading (and its sub-tree) to the selected location.

Looks and feels like Xcode's "Open Quickly" (⌘⇧O). Single NSTextField + filtered list view.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, this is the Mac-native form of org-refile. Apple Notes' "Move Note…", Things' "Move to project…", OmniFocus reorganize-by-keyboard all use this pattern. Drag-and-drop in the sidebar handles whole-file moves; refile-by-picker is for sub-heading granularity where dragging is awkward.

Trick to get right: when the moved heading is the target of `[[wikilinks]]` from other notes, either update the links transparently or surface the would-be breakage and ask. Don't silently break links.
acceptance_criteria:
- ⌘⇧M opens the picker only when the caret is on a heading
- Fuzzy match across all (file, heading-path) tuples in the vault
- Move writes both source and destination atomically
- Wikilinks targeting the moved heading either auto-update or surface a confirmation dialog
- Undo restores the heading to its original location and rolls back any link updates
notes: UI tests added (RefileUITests): picker-opens-on-heading, cross-note move (asserts editor content moved), and link-dialog appears+updates. Test target compiles (build-for-testing OK). NOTE: this sandbox can't run XCUITest — 'Timed out while enabling automation mode' (automation-permission limit), so the refile UI tests must be run on a real session/CI. Pure logic (HeadingOutline/HeadingRefile/HeadingRefileLinks/RefilePlan) fully unit-tested. Awaiting a UI-test run (Mark's machine or CI) before close.
close_reason: Refile implemented and verified end-to-end. Pure core (HeadingOutline/HeadingRefile/HeadingRefileLinks/RefilePlan) unit-tested; ⌘⇧M picker + inline link-impact confirmation + multi-file write + undo wired. RefileUITests (3) all PASS on a real session: picker-opens-on-heading, cross-note move (asserts content moved off source and onto dest), link dialog appears + rewrites [[Note#Heading]]. Also fixed the UI-test harness to open a vault window deterministically under --ui-testing (was relying on state restoration). UX: stays in source after refile (org-style).

title: Capture templates (filterable picker on quick-capture)
description:
Templates are markdown files in `.punkrecords/templates/` with frontmatter declaring fields (title, body shape, destination directory, optional summarize_url binding). Ship with defaults: Note, Journal entry, Meeting note, Web summary (URL field), Task, Idea.

Quick-capture composer (PUNK-5pr) becomes a two-stage UI: first keystrokes filter the template list; selecting a template (or enter on empty input → default "Note") opens its structured form.

Users author new templates by writing markdown files in the templates directory — no DSL, no special editor.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, this is the Mac-native form of org-capture. Pattern is familiar from Raycast / Alfred / Drafts / Things Quick Entry. The Web summary template is the bridge between the capture surface and the URL→summary epic (PUNK-6rh).
acceptance_criteria:
- Quick-capture panel shows template list filtered by typed text
- Selecting a template opens its form; submit writes the file to the template's destination
- Templates are plain markdown files; user can author/edit them from the editor
- "Web summary" template triggers summarize_url (PUNK-ddq) and opens the resulting note
- Default ship-list: Note, Journal entry, Meeting note, Web summary, Task, Idea

title: Heading folding in editor (outline disclosure)
description:
Editor supports heading folding via disclosure indicators. ⌘. toggles the heading under the caret; ⌘⌥. folds all. Folded ranges visually collapse but remain searchable (find/replace + FTS still hit folded content).

Why: table-stakes for any markdown editor with structured documents. SwiftUI OutlineGroup or NSTextLayoutManager range-hiding makes this nearly free. Bear, Craft, iA Writer, Pages, Apple Notes all ship this — per the translation guide, "just don't skip it."

Implementation is editor-type-specific so this depends on the upcoming markdown-editor decision (TextKit 2 vs WKWebView+CodeMirror etc.).
acceptance_criteria:
- Disclosure chevron beside each heading
- ⌘. toggles the current heading
- ⌘⌥. folds all headings in the document
- Folded text is excluded from visual range but still hit by ⌘F find and by FTS
- Fold state is per-document and persists across editor reopen (session, not on-disk)
notes: Salvage state 2026-07-08: a prior agent run (killed by the monthly spend limit) left a 193-line Core fold-state skeleton at Packages/PunkRecordsCore/Sources/PunkRecordsCore/Services/SectionFolding.swift, preserved UNMERGED on branch worktree-agent-a704442ef733690bf (commit: see branch tip; base = main at the PUNK-zp1 merge). Remaining work: compose section folding with LivePreviewDecorator's null-glyph pass (section folds are toggle-driven, NOT caret-driven, and may extend beyond the visible scanRange — keep as a separate pass/state), chevron affordance, ⌘./⌘⌥. menu wiring (mind macOS cancel semantics for ⌘.), caret-escape rule so typing never edits invisible text, per-document session fold registry, unit tests. Reuse HeadingOutline for section ranges.

title: Sparse-tree sidebar search (Xcode Project Navigator pattern)
description:
Vault-wide search field at the top of the sidebar. As the user types, the outline filters: matching headings stay visible, ancestors stay visible to preserve context, everything else collapses. Parent nodes show hit-count badges (Mail unread-counts style).

⌘⇧F focuses the sidebar field. ⌘F still does in-document find. Click a result → editor opens the file scrolled and highlighted at the match.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, this is the Mac-native form of Org-mode's sparse-tree search — a pattern Mac users already know from Xcode Project Navigator, Finder column filter, Mail filtered threads. Hierarchical context (file → H1 → H2 → matched H3) is the whole point; flat result lists lose the meaning of the match.

Implementation: SwiftUI OutlineGroup driven by a filtered data source backed by the existing FTS5 index for relevance ranking.
acceptance_criteria:
- ⌘⇧F focuses sidebar search
- Filter live (debounced ≤50ms) on a 10k-note vault
- Ancestors of matches stay visible; non-matching siblings collapse
- Hit-count badges on parent folders
- Click a result jumps to the file scrolled to the matched line, with the match highlighted
close_reason: Shipped in 2a7d4d9: SidebarFilter pure-function module in Core (11 passing unit tests covering case/diacritic insensitivity, hierarchical context, hit-count correctness, 10k-doc perf floor at 22ms). VaultBrowserView gained a search field with Cmd+Shift+F focus, auto-expanded DisclosureGroups during filter, hit-count badges. Modal SearchView (FTS5 body search) relocated to Cmd+Option+F as 'Find by Content…' so the two search surfaces have distinct keybindings.

title: Smart Notes — saved searches via NSPredicateEditor
description:
Sidebar section listing saved structured queries. "New Smart Note…" opens a sheet using NSPredicateEditor (the Finder / Mail / Photos rule-builder). Predicates compose over: tag, status, frontmatter key, date created/modified, scheduled, due, full-text match.

Ship with built-in smart notes: Inbox, Today, This Week, Untagged, Recently Captured, Web Summaries.

Persistence: each smart note is a markdown file in `Smart Notes/{name}.md` with frontmatter holding the predicate as a serialized structure. Plain-text on disk, Mac-native rule-builder UI on top.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, no PKM in the competitive set surfaces the macOS-native rule-builder for saved searches — they ship flat keyword strings or community plugins. NSPredicateEditor gives us the most usable structured-query UI in the category for ~3 days of wiring. This is the demo screenshot.
acceptance_criteria:
- Sidebar "Smart Notes" section populated with built-in queries
- "New Smart Note" sheet uses NSPredicateEditor with templates for the supported fields
- Today smart note correctly surfaces headings with `scheduled <= today AND status != done`
- Smart notes persist across launches as plain-text Smart Notes/{name}.md files
- Predicate is editable round-trip (open existing smart note → re-edit in sheet)
close_reason: Merged to main (b8cc16d). Agent run was killed by the monthly spend limit at the final build step; orchestrator salvaged the worktree, fixed one lint violation (failable Data→String in SmartNoteQuery.toJSON), and validated everything: 561 Core tests (43 new: query AST round-trip incl. unknown-version rejection, NSPredicate bridge, evaluator incl. per-heading Today rule 'scheduled <= today AND status != done', file round-trip, built-ins), 140 integration tests, app build, swiftlint --strict 0, architecture check clean. Design: codable SmartNoteQuery AST (NSPredicate only at the AppKit edge), Smart Notes/{name}.md persistence, six documented built-ins (Inbox=untagged vault-root, Today, This Week, Untagged, Recently Captured=7d, Web Summaries=Web/ or #web), sidebar section above chats, NSPredicateEditor sheet. Manual: sheet/predicate-editor visuals.

title: Inspector panel (⌘I) for per-heading metadata
description:
SwiftUI inspector pane bound to the heading under the caret. Editable fields: tags, status (todo/doing/done), scheduled, due, custom key-value rows.

On save, writes a `> [!props]` markdown callout block immediately under the heading. For document-root metadata, appends to YAML frontmatter instead.

Why: this is the schema surface that unblocks Smart Notes (predicates query against this), DataDetectors (Today smart note pulls scheduled), and any structured-query feature. Per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md this is Days 1–2 of the 2-week sprint — everything else depends on it.

UX reference: mirrors Reminders.app task detail (title, notes, due, priority, tags, list). Date fields accept natural-language input via NSDataDetector ("next Monday", "+1w", "tomorrow at 3").

Key decision: hard-code status to todo/doing/done (3 states). Custom per-file state sets are an Emacs-tier configurability trap — explicitly cut by the translation guide.
acceptance_criteria:
- ⌘I toggles inspector on the heading under the caret
- Edits round-trip to `> [!props]` callout (or frontmatter at doc root) without breaking markdown rendering
- Date fields parse "next Monday" / "tomorrow" / "+1w" via NSDataDetector
- Caret moving to a different heading updates the inspector live
- Three statuses: todo, doing, done — hard-coded, no user config
close_reason: Merged to main, validated by orchestrator: Core PropsBlock ('> key:: value' Dataview-style callout lines, lossless idempotent round-trip), HeadingProps document surgery (insert/replace/remove under heading via HeadingOutline; frontmatter at doc root; UTF-16 semantics, EOF/newline-drift/lazy-blockquote handling), NaturalDateParser (+1w/+3d grammar, today/tomorrow/next Monday with 'at <time>', NSDataDetector fallback, injected now). 37 new Core tests (503 total green post-merge). App: InspectorPanel + AppState+Inspector write-back mirroring refile path (repo save, session/search upsert, NSUndoManager 'Edit Metadata', error surfacing); cmd-I in Editor menu; .inspector on VaultWindow; RawEditorView untouched. Build + 140 integration tests + swiftlint --strict green post-merge. Manual-only: inspector layout/theming. Known UX wart filed as follow-up: caret resets to 0 on save (editorReloadToken reload), so inspector re-binds to doc root after each save.

title: Wikilinks autocomplete + backlinks panel
description:
Two-part feature on top of the existing MarkdownParser wikilink support.

(1) Autocomplete: typing `[[` in the editor pops a list of matching note titles (fuzzy match on title + aliases); selecting one inserts the wikilink. Keyboard-driven (↑/↓/enter/esc).

(2) Backlinks panel: collapsible section at the bottom of every note showing all notes that link to the current note, with a 1-line context excerpt. Click jumps to the source.

Underlying: SQLite link-graph table updated on file save. Columns: source_path, target_path, target_title, source_line, context_excerpt. Index on target_path. Repository emits VaultChange.linksUpdated so the panel refreshes reactively.
acceptance_criteria:
- Typing `[[` shows autocomplete within 50ms on a 10k-note vault
- Backlinks panel renders within 100ms on note open
- Renaming a note updates link graph and surfaces broken links
- Unresolved [[New Note]] links are visually distinct and click-to-create
notes:
SCOPE NARROWED (PunkRecords_Wysiwyg_research.md ingestion): the in-editor wikilink autocomplete and pill-chip rendering portion of this issue is now owned by PUNK-6du (Editor W4: Wikilink pill chips + tag chips + in-editor autocomplete) as part of the markdown editor epic PUNK-1hy.

What remains in this issue:
- Backlinks panel as a collapsible UI section at the bottom of every note
- Underlying SQLite link-graph table (source_path, target_path, target_title, source_line, context_excerpt) maintained on save
- VaultChange.linksUpdated reactive refresh
- Renaming a note updates link graph; broken links surfaced

The link-graph table is also the data source for the unresolved-link styling and click-to-create flow in PUNK-6du — coordinate so both issues share one schema.
close_reason: Shipped reactive refresh via AppState.vaultChangeTick + count badge in panel header. The autocomplete half of this issue migrated to PUNK-6du (Editor W4) per the editor research; backlinks panel + link-graph (SQLiteSearchIndex.backlinks) was already implemented, this commit adds the missing reactive refresh that prompted the issue.

title: Global quick-capture hotkey
description:
System-wide hotkey opens a floating composer; on submit, a new .md note is written to the vault with auto-frontmatter (id, created, source: quick-capture). Goal: <300ms from hotkey to text cursor.

Why: capture friction kills PKMs. If writing a thought down costs more than one keystroke, the thought dies. This is the cheapest, highest-leverage P0 we can ship.

Implementation notes: NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents for the hotkey; floating SwiftUI window (NSPanel + .floatingPanel style); on submit, write via the existing repository actor; synchronously add to FTS5 index so the note is searchable immediately. Hotkey is configurable in Settings; default ⌃⌥⌘N.
acceptance_criteria:
- Hotkey from any app opens composer in <300ms
- Submit writes file + indexes synchronously
- Esc dismisses without saving
- Hotkey is rebindable; conflicts surfaced to user
notes: When implementing: the Settings window (SettingsView.swift) already has a Keyboard tab — host the hotkey configuration + system-shortcut conflict detection there (was part of PUNK-yf7, closed; hotkey UI deferred to this issue).

title: Web-summary note schema + writer + paste-URL trigger
description:
Storage + UX entry point for the URL→summary feature.

Schema (Web/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md): YAML frontmatter with title, source_url, source_domain, author, published, fetched (ISO8601), fetcher (tier name), summary_model, summary_prompt_version, word_count, reading_time_min, tags, type: web-summary, status, links (wikilinks).

Consider whether implementation-detail fields (fetcher, summary_model, summary_prompt_version) should live under a _punkrecords: namespace to keep top-level frontmatter clean for users. Recommend doing so.

UX triggers:
- Detect URL in chat composer; show inline "Summarize this URL" affordance
- Command palette / menu: "Summarize URL from clipboard"
- Future: share extension and quick-capture URL paste path

Progress UI: live status (fetching → extracting → summarizing → writing) with a Cancel button.
acceptance_criteria:
- Paste URL into chat → inline button appears → click → progress UI → note created in Web/ within 10s for a typical article
- Frontmatter validates against schema (snapshot test)
- _punkrecords: namespace keeps user-visible frontmatter clean
notes: PUNK-tlu landed the full pipeline as standalone Core services under Services/WebSummary/. Integration recipe: WebSummaryPrompt.build(content:) → TextCompleter.complete → WebSummaryPostProcessor.process(rawResponse:content:) → note body; store WebSummaryPrompt.promptVersion + WebSummaryValidator issues/unresolvedCitationCount in frontmatter. No existing summarize seam was reused (ChatSessionController+Summarize summarizes conversations, not fetched pages).
close_reason: Merged to main (31b2703). Agent was killed by the spend limit while writing the status view; orchestrator salvaged the worktree and completed it: composer 'Summarize this URL' affordance + URLSummaryStatusView progress row (fetching→summarizing→writing labels honest to the actual pipeline: fetch+extract is one opaque call) with working Cancel via Task cancellation; 'Summarize URL from Clipboard' Editor-menu command (⌘⇧U) via the vault-window notification rail; also fixed a 12-param lint violation by bundling buildFrontmatter inputs into a struct. Core: WebSummaryNoteWriter (Web/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md, slug truncation + numeric collision suffixing, user-facing frontmatter + _punkrecords: namespaced implementation fields incl. summary_prompt_version/word_count/reading_time/validator counts, YAML escaping for arbitrary titles) and ComposerURLDetector (lone-URL rule). Pipeline: ThreeTierWebContentFetcher → WebSummaryPrompt → DeferredSessionTextCompleter (provider-agnostic) → PostProcessor → Validator → writer → repo save + session upsert + search index + select. LanguageModelFactory.modelIdentifier added for summary_model attribution. Validated: 594 Core / 293 Infra / 140 integration tests, build, swiftlint --strict, arch check all green. Manual: affordance/status visuals and the live 10s end-to-end criterion (needs a real API key + network).

title: Structured summary prompt + citation rendering
description:
Prompt + post-processor that takes WebFetchTool output and produces the 4-section markdown body with inline citations.

Output sections:
1. TL;DR (1–3 sentences)
2. Key points (5–9 bullets, each with inline [¹] citation)
3. Notable quotes (2–4 verbatim excerpts ≤30 words)
4. Why this matters / open questions (optional)

Citation strategy: for each bullet/quote, the LLM emits a JSON tuple { citation_index, supporting_text, nearest_heading_anchor }. Post-processor converts to #:~:text=<url-encoded supporting_text>; if supporting_text is non-unique on the page, prepend prefix-/-suffix per W3C scroll-to-text-fragment spec.

Must be deterministic enough that re-summarizing the same URL with the same model + prompt_version produces identical citations (modulo LLM nondeterminism). Store prompt_version in frontmatter.

Provider-agnostic: works with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Apple Foundation Models. Use structured outputs / tool-call schema where available.
acceptance_criteria:
- Given a long-form article, output passes structural validation: 4 sections, every bullet has a citation, every citation resolves to a unique passage on the page
- Snapshot tests across 5 reference articles pin the structural output (eval-tagged)
- Re-running with same prompt_version + model produces byte-equal Sources section in ≥80% of trials
close_reason: Merged to main (10533c1), validated by orchestrator: WebSummaryPrompt (promptVersion web-summary-v1), Codable payload models, TextFragmentBuilder (W3C scroll-to-text-fragment: unique/prefix-suffix-disambiguated/notFound/ambiguous outcomes; spec-correct percent-encoding; agent found+fixed a real non-overlapping-scan undercount bug), WebSummaryPostProcessor (JSON→4-section markdown, footnote citations, >30-word quotes truncated), WebSummaryValidator (structure + citation groundedness). 44 new deterministic tests; 466 Core tests green post-merge. Deferred per scope: 5-article snapshot evals + live structural eval suite; PUNK-ddq wires prompt→completer→postprocessor and stores promptVersion in frontmatter (integration recipe recorded in that issue's notes).

title: WebFetchTool: three-tier extraction ladder (SwiftSoup → WKWebView → Jina)
description:
Build the fetcher that turns a URL into clean, structured markdown for downstream summarization.

Tier 1 (default, offline, private): URLSession + SwiftSoup + Readability-style scoring ported to Swift (~400 lines). Fast, no JS rendering.
Tier 2 (fallback for JS-rendered or sparse pages): invisible WKWebView; inject @mozilla/readability after load; evaluate parse function. Detect trigger via isProbablyReaderable() returning false OR Tier 1 content < ~250 chars.
Tier 3 (escape hatch, opt-in only): Jina Reader API (r.jina.ai/<URL>) — explicit user consent dialog noting URL leaves the device.

Output shape: { title, byline, contentMarkdown, headings: [(level, text, anchor_id)], extractedAt, tier, sourceURL, canonicalURL }. The headings array drives citation fallback. Cache HTML in Web/_cache/{slug}.html for offline anchor rendering.

Shares ground with PUNK-e5u (which scoped a similar tool for web_search fallback) — coordinate so we have one WebFetchTool, not two.
acceptance_criteria:
- Given 20 sample URLs (mix of blogs, docs sites, SPAs, news), Tier 1 succeeds on >70% within 1s
- Tier 2 triggers automatically when Tier 1 underperforms; succeeds on JS-rendered pages
- Tier 3 requires per-URL user consent; consent persisted per-domain
- Output preserves heading structure precisely (each h1–h3 with anchor id or generated slug)
close_reason: Three-tier ladder shipped: Tier 1 URLSession+SwiftSoup Readability-port (offline, private), Tier 2 WKWebView + vendored Mozilla Readability.js (Apache-2.0, headers intact), Tier 3 Jina Reader gated by per-domain consent (pure Core policy + thin NSAlert). WebContent output model with anchor-preserving headings; HTML cache at Web/_cache; web_fetch AgentTool wired into ChatSessionController. 78 offline tests; manual Tier-1 sanity on Wikipedia/PG/CodingHorror preserved real anchor ids. Validated on merged main: arch OK, strict lint 0, 40+38+99+11 tests. Pending: live 20-URL benchmark, Tier 2 end-to-end browser pass, consent dialog visuals (manual).

title: Epic: URL → cliffs-notes web summary feature
description:
Wedge feature: user pastes a URL → agent fetches the page → produces a structured summary (TL;DR + key points + quotes) → stored as a markdown note in the vault with per-bullet citations that link back to the source paragraph via #:~:text= fragments.

Why: differentiator versus Bear/Apple Notes/Obsidian; no existing tool combines (a) one-paste ingest, (b) structured summary, (c) per-bullet jump-to-source, (d) plain-markdown storage. Recall.it is closest but cloud-only.

Scope tracked via sub-issues: fetcher ladder, summary prompt, citation rendering, storage schema/UI, failure modes. See PunkRecords_Features_Research.md §3.
acceptance_criteria:
- Paste a URL anywhere it makes sense (chat composer, quick-capture, command palette) and get a markdown note created in Web/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md within 10s for a typical article
- Note has TL;DR + 5–9 key points + 2–4 quotes + Sources section
- Each bullet has an inline numbered citation [¹] resolving to a #:~:text= URL that, when opened in Safari, scrolls to the cited paragraph
- Works offline-first for static pages; falls back to WKWebView for JS-heavy pages; offers Jina Reader as a final tier with explicit user consent
close_reason: All three sub-issues delivered and merged (PUNK-tlu structured summary + scroll-to-text citations, PUNK-ddq Web/{date}-{slug}.md writer + composer affordance + clipboard command + progress UI with cancel, PUNK-zup failure-mode routing: PDF page citations, YouTube transcript timestamp citations, paywall/login typed blocks, transcript prompt variant, long-form chunk plan). Pipeline is provider-agnostic via the TextCompleter seam and offline-first via the three-tier ladder with consent-gated Jina. Remaining MANUAL verification: a live end-to-end run (paste URL → note within 10s; citations scroll in Safari) needs a real API key + network — not automated per CLAUDE.md discipline. Residual engineering tracked in PUNK-w94.

title: Preserve editor caret/scroll position across programmatic saves (editorReloadToken reload)
description: Found during PUNK-4bz: any programmatic document rewrite (inspector applyProps, refile, undo restore) bumps editorReloadToken, which reloads the editor and resets the caret to 0 and scroll to top. Consequence: after saving a props edit, the ⌘I inspector re-binds to the document root instead of the heading being edited, so setting several fields on one heading means re-selecting it each time. Fix direction: RawEditorView/DocumentEditorViewModel should capture (selectedRange, visibleRect) before applying a programmatic content swap and restore them after (clamped to new length), or apply the edit as a ranged replaceCharacters through the textStorage instead of a full reload. Acceptance: applyProps on a heading keeps the caret inside that heading's section; inspector stays bound to the same heading across consecutive field edits; undo restore also preserves position.

title: Provider-matrix live evals: run scenario suites across anthropic/openai/ollama/foundationmodels
description:
LiveMusicDSPEvals now honors PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_PROVIDER (anthropic default | openai | ollama | foundationmodels) for single-provider runs. This issue is the full matrix: parameterize the live suites so one invocation measures all AVAILABLE providers and reports per-provider results side by side.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- Use Swift Testing parameterization: @Test(arguments: LLMProviderID.allCases) (or a curated list) on the scenario bodies; inside, resolve via LanguageModelFactory.makeModel and SKIP-not-fail when a provider is unavailable (no key in keychain, no Ollama daemon on /api/tags, no Apple Intelligence) — reuse the VaultEvalSkip pattern.
- Providers differ in tool-calling reliability: keep hard assertions provider-agnostic (grounding, tool usage, note creation) and record per-provider metrics in the transcript filename (e.g. s2-filter-synthesis.openai.md).
- Ollama guidance: default local model must fit dev machines; read the configured model from the same settings seam the app uses (PUNK-yw1 will centralize this — coordinate).
- Persist per-provider EvalReports through EvalResultStore so VariantComparator can diff providers, not just prompt variants (the comparator is variant-keyed; a provider can BE a variant label, e.g. 'anthropic-current').
- Cost control: matrix runs stay behind PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1; document expected cost per full matrix in the suite doc comment.
acceptance_criteria: One command runs the DSP scenario set against every available provider, skipping unavailable ones cleanly; transcripts and EvalReports are written per provider; a comparison summary (even print-based) shows per-provider pass counts and token estimates.

title: Chrome MV3 shell + native messaging host binary
description:
Chrome/Chromium counterpart of the Safari shell: the same shared core packaged as an MV3 extension, talking to the app through a Chrome native-messaging host.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- Native messaging host: a small Swift executable (new SPM target, e.g. punkrecords-nmhost) speaking Chrome's stdio framing (4-byte little-endian length + JSON) and forwarding to the PUNK-ce7 socket. Keep the framing codec a pure function with unit tests (malformed length, oversize message, partial reads).
- Host manifest JSON installed to ~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/NativeMessagingHosts/com.markpauley.punkrecords.json with allowed_origins pinned to the extension id; the app writes/repairs this manifest on launch (Settings toggle), since the extension id is only stable once packed.
- MV3 specifics: background service worker (not persistent page); chrome.sidePanel available for the chat issue; keep permissions minimal (activeTab, contextMenus, scripting, nativeMessaging).
- Unpacked-extension dev flow documented; no Web Store requirement for personal use.
acceptance_criteria: From Chrome: page and selection capture land notes in a test vault via the host binary. Framing codec unit-tested. Manifest install/repair works from app Settings. Extension functions with app cold-launch.

title: Safari Web Extension shell (app-embedded, native messaging to the IPC rail)
description:
Safari app-extension target hosting the shared WebExtension core, distributed inside the PunkRecords app bundle.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- New target in project.yml: a Safari Web Extension appex (SFSafariWebExtensionHandler) embedding the built core bundle as resources; remember xcodegen generate after editing project.yml.
- The handler receives browser.runtime.sendNativeMessage payloads and forwards them over the PUNK-ce7 socket to the running app (launch-if-needed via bundle id). Keep the handler a dumb forwarder — protocol types come from Core.
- Signing: personal team is fine for local use; extension enables via Safari Settings > Extensions (document the toggle + 'Allow Unsigned Extensions' dev path in README).
- Reuse Safari-specific wins where cheap: if the page has Reader available, Safari's extracted article can be preferred over in-tab Readability (PUNK-5xp bonus note applies here too).
acceptance_criteria: Toolbar button in Safari captures the current page into a test vault end-to-end (content script -> handler -> socket -> note on disk). Selection context-menu capture works. Cold-launch path verified with the app quit.

title: Shared WebExtension core: in-tab extraction, selection anchors, destination picker
description:
The single TypeScript/JS WebExtension codebase both browser shells load (Safari Web Extensions and Chrome MV3 both speak the browser.*/chrome.* API; keep to the common subset + polyfill).

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- Content script: bundle @mozilla/readability; extract title/byline/content from the LIVE DOM (this is the whole advantage: logged-in pages, paywalls, SPAs come for free). Convert to markdown (turndown or similar) — output shape mirrors PUNK-ibh: {title, byline, contentMarkdown, headings[], sourceURL, canonicalURL}.
- Selection capture: context-menu item 'Capture selection to PunkRecords'; compute a #:~:text= text-fragment anchor for the selection (same citation scheme as PUNK-6rh) plus surrounding-paragraph context.
- Popup UI: destination picker (autocomplete backed by the suggestDestinations IPC endpoint), template choice (align with PUNK-37e), capture-whole-page vs selection, and a sticky 'collecting for: <project>' mode that biases destinationHint.
- Keyboard shortcut command for one-keystroke capture.
- Keep transport abstract: a thin Port interface the Safari shell backs with native messaging to the extension handler and the Chrome shell backs with a native-messaging host. No direct network calls anywhere.
- Repo layout suggestion: BrowserExtension/ at repo root (core/ + safari/ + chrome/), built with a plain esbuild script; do NOT entangle with xcodegen until the Safari shell issue.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fixture page, the core extracts clean markdown with headings preserved; selection capture yields text + working text-fragment URL; popup renders picker fed by a mocked Port; both shells can load the built bundle unchanged.

title: App-local capture/agent IPC endpoint (shared rail for share extension + browser extensions)
description:
A local IPC surface on the running app that external capture clients (PUNK-5xp share extension, browser extensions) call to (a) create/steer captures, (b) query note titles for destination autocomplete, (c) run agent chat turns with streaming events.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- Transport: Unix domain socket in the app container (PUNK-5xp already sketches this; keep ONE implementation). JSON-lines protocol; each request carries an auth token stored in the app group / keychain so arbitrary local processes can't write the vault.
- Endpoints (v1): capture {url, title, markdown, selection?, anchors?, destinationHint?} -> {notePath}; suggestDestinations {query} -> [titles/folders] (back with QuickOpenMatcher over AppState.documents); chat {prompt, pageContext?} -> streamed AgentEvent JSON (map from SessionAgentRunner events; toolStart/toolEnd/textToken/done).
- Cold-launch: if the app is not running, client launches it via bundle id (LSOpenURL / open -b) then retries connect with backoff — mirror PUNK-5xp acceptance.
- Capture writes go through the existing repository actor + VaultPaths conventions; index synchronously so the note is immediately searchable (same requirement as PUNK-5pr).
- Keep protocol types in Core (pure, unit-testable encode/decode), socket plumbing in Infra, wiring in App. Per CLAUDE.md discipline: protocol codec + destination-suggestion logic are pure functions with unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: A CLI test client can: capture a note into a temp vault via the socket, get destination suggestions, and stream a scripted-model chat turn (using --ui-testing-scripted-chat wiring or ScriptedLanguageModel) — all with auth enforced. Codec round-trip unit tests pass.

title: Epic: Browser capture extensions (Safari + Chrome) — in-page capture, steering, and vault chat
description:
Reading-companion extensions that capture web content into the vault from inside the browser, with agent-steered placement. Complements the capture trinity (PUNK-5pr hotkey, PUNK-37e templates, PUNK-5xp share extension, PUNK-tjl intents) and the URL-summary epic (PUNK-6rh). The extension's unique value over the share extension: (1) the page is ALREADY rendered and authenticated — a content script running Readability in-tab beats the whole PUNK-ibh fetch ladder for paywalled/logged-in/SPA pages; (2) selection capture with #:~:text= text-fragment anchors matching PUNK-6rh citations; (3) an in-browser agent chat for capture-time steering and have-I-seen-this-before queries against the vault.

ARCHITECTURE PRINCIPLE: the extension stays dumb (extract, transport, picker UI); ALL intelligence lives in the app's session path (SessionAgentRunner + tools + vault conventions). Extensions are additional clients of the same local IPC rail the share extension uses. One shared WebExtension core (browser.* API), two thin shells.

Sub-issues: IPC endpoint, shared core, Safari shell, Chrome shell + native host, agent chat panel. Epic closes when a capture from both browsers lands a well-placed note and chat works in at least one browser.
acceptance_criteria: From Safari and Chrome: one click captures the current page (or selection) into the vault as markdown with source URL + anchors, honoring a destination picked in the popup or steered by the agent. Chat panel answers vault queries about the open page in at least one browser. No vault writes happen except through the app.

title: LiveMusicDSPEvals: real-vault live eval suite (grounding, synthesis, hallucination, capture-steering scenarios)
description: Add an opt-in live eval suite that runs the session agent against an ARBITRARY on-disk vault (env: PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_VAULT) instead of in-memory fixtures, with six scenarios grounded in the Music DSP research vault: grounded retrieval, cross-note synthesis (Chiba filters x VA filter papers), negative-knowledge/hallucination probe (wavefolding — verified absent), linked-note creation with resolvable wikilinks, June-2026 digest generation, and steered capture/refile judgment. Follows the LiveSessionAgentEvals direct-runner pattern (FileSystemDocumentRepository + SQLiteSearchIndex + SessionAgentRunner), gated on PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1 and skipping cleanly when PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_VAULT is unset. Never points at the real vault by default — run against a disposable copy since CreateNoteTool writes. Addresses eval-gap findings: hallucination/grounding coverage, wikilink resolution in created notes, multi-hop tool chains on the live path.
acceptance_criteria: Suite skips (not fails) without both env vars. Each scenario prints a delimited transcript (prompt, tool calls, final text) and writes a per-scenario markdown transcript to PUNKRECORDS_EVAL_OUTPUT when set. Wikilink assertions verify link targets exist in the vault on disk. Wavefolding probe asserts the agent admits absence rather than fabricating.
notes: First run 2026-07-03 against a copy of ~/Documents/Vaults (Research Vault + Chiba, 97 notes): 2 pass / 4 fail. S1 clean pass — correct retrieval, correct attribution to the 2001 granular synthesis paper, all answer wikilinks resolve. S3 passed on a rubric FALSE POSITIVE: the admission keyword 'nothing' matched inside a truncated planning preamble, not a real admission — tighten the rubric to also require the run to have ended with a text-only round once PUNK-dpl lands. S2/S4/S5/S6 all failed for one shared root cause, now filed as PUNK-dpl (runner ends loop on narration text accompanying tool calls). Secondary observation from S5: the model searched 'papers added June 2026' — FTS5 cannot answer recency queries; the tool surface lacks date-filtered listing (list_documents has no metadata filters). Consider a follow-up tool enhancement. Suite + transcripts prove the real-vault eval approach pays for itself: first run found a P1 shipping bug.
close_reason: Suite landed (Tests/PunkRecordsEvalTests/LiveMusicDSPEvals.swift, commit 9d8095b) and paid for itself immediately: first runs found PUNK-dpl and PUNK-xu7, both now fixed and verified by the suite at 6/6. Transcripts include tool args, tool results (with error flags), and final text. Rubric-tightening ideas recorded in PUNK-x0y.

title: Live Preview phase 2: block affordances — clickable checkboxes, drawn horizontal rules, blockquote bars, code-fence chrome
description:
Second Live Preview slice: block-level widgets drawn over folded source. Depends on the PUNK-ef9 folding primitive.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
1. Task-list checkboxes. Pure hit-test checkboxTarget(at:in:) that finds '- [ ]' / '- [x]' items (also * and + bullets, nested indentation), modeled on WikilinkDecorator.tagTarget, with unit tests. Clicking toggles the x — this is a REAL text edit: route through shouldChangeText(in:replacementString:) + replaceCharacters + didChangeText exactly like slashCommandSelected in RawEditorView.swift, so undo and the 2s save debounce work. Rendering: fold the bracket span and draw a checkbox (SF Symbol image or NSBezierPath) from the layout manager at the folded range's line-fragment rect — WikilinkPillLayoutManager.drawBackground shows the enclosing-rect technique; a checked state may also apply strikethrough/dim to the item text.
2. Horizontal rules. HybridUXDecorator.horizontalRuleRegex already matches the line; fold it and draw a 1px separator across the text container width in drawBackground using theme.horizontalRuleColor.
3. Blockquotes. Fold the leading > markers and draw a left vertical bar per enclosing rect in the blockquote color (theme decoratorStyle.blockquoteColor).
4. Code fences. v1: keep fence lines visible but fully dimmed, and draw a block background behind the fenced range (theme codeBackground). Do NOT fold fence lines yet — the language tag must stay editable/discoverable. A language chip / copy button is an optional follow-up.
5. Per CLAUDE.md Testing discipline: every transform and hit-test is a pure function with unit tests; visual placement of drawn widgets is an explicitly-manual check — say so in the close reason.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a checkbox toggles its state through the undo-safe edit path and persists via the save debounce. Checkbox hit-testing and toggle transform are pure, unit-tested functions. Horizontal rules render as drawn lines and blockquotes show a left bar with markers folded; caret entry reveals raw source for each. Fenced code blocks get a block background with fences dimmed, not folded.

title: Add automated architecture-boundary checks
description: Design analysis finding: the repository has a clear clean architecture contract in CLAUDE.md, and the current package manifests mostly honor it. That contract is currently social/documented rather than automatically checked. Principle: Code Health Guardian code-health guardrails and Pragmatic Programmer automation; make important design constraints executable. Evidence: Core should never import App/Infra/FoundationModels/AnyLanguageModel, App can compose Core+Infra, and Infra should bridge integrations. Suggested direction: add a lightweight script or test that scans imports/package manifests for forbidden dependency directions and optionally verifies generated project assumptions that commonly drift after project.yml changes.
acceptance_criteria: A local quality gate fails if Core imports App/Infra/AnyLanguageModel/FoundationModels or if package/project dependency direction regresses; the check is documented and can run without live API credentials.
close_reason: Scripts/check-architecture.sh added: offline gate failing on Core→Infra/App/FoundationModels/AnyLanguageModel imports, Infra→App imports, and Core manifest regressions. Verified it catches injected violations and passes on clean tree. Documented in CLAUDE.md Build Commands. Merged at fbdc480.

title: Align README build/test docs with the actual Xcode 26 workflow
description: Design analysis finding: CLAUDE.md and project.yml describe the real contract: macOS/Xcode 26, xcodegen after project changes, and -skipMacroValidation for command-line xcodebuild. README.md still says macOS 15, Xcode 16, and shows xcodebuild without -skipMacroValidation. Principle: Pragmatic Programmer broken windows and Ousterhout obscurity; setup instructions are part of the system interface. Evidence: README.md Requirements/Building conflict with CLAUDE.md Build Commands and project.yml deployment target 26.0. Suggested direction: update README requirements, command-line build/test examples, live eval caveat, SwiftLint expectation, and generated-project note so first-run instructions match current reality.
acceptance_criteria: README.md requirements and build/test/lint commands match CLAUDE.md and project.yml; command-line examples include -skipMacroValidation; live eval/API cost caveat is visible; stale macOS 15/Xcode 16 references are removed or explained.
close_reason: README requirements/build/test/lint docs aligned with CLAUDE.md + project.yml: macOS 26/Xcode 26, -skipMacroValidation, xcodegen generated-project note, TEST_RUNNER_ live-eval caveat, SwiftLint section. Merged as 971afa5.

title: Introduce a vault-session boundary to reduce AppState coupling
description: Design analysis finding: AppState is both dependency container and workflow coordinator: it owns concrete Infra types, opens vaults, heals duplicate IDs, rebuilds indexes, starts file watching, creates/renames/deletes notes, updates the search index, and carries UI-only state. Principle: Ousterhout information hiding and Pragmatic Programmer orthogonality; vault lifecycle and document workflows should have a smaller public interface so views do not need to know infrastructure shape. Evidence: Sources/PunkRecordsApp/App/AppState.swift stores FileSystemDocumentRepository, SQLiteSearchIndex, NoteCompiler, KeychainService and implements vault open plus CRUD/index update flows. Suggested direction: create a VaultSession or VaultServices facade that exposes Core protocol-shaped operations and streams, while AppState owns selection/presentation state and delegates vault workflows.
acceptance_criteria: AppState no longer exposes concrete repository/search implementation types to views; vault open/create/rename/delete/watch logic sits behind a focused session facade; integration tests cover the facade behavior currently embedded in AppState.

title: Split RawEditorView coordinator by editor responsibility
description: Design analysis finding: RawEditorView is a 663-line file whose NSViewRepresentable/Coordinator owns TextKit construction, theme application, syntax highlighting, hybrid decoration, wikilink/tag completion, slash commands, Emacs state, autosave debounce, selection mirroring, and context menu wiring. Principle: Ousterhout change amplification and cognitive load; unrelated editor concerns should be independently understandable and testable. Evidence: Sources/PunkRecordsApp/Views/RawEditorView.swift has EditorTextViewRepresentable with a broad initializer surface and a Coordinator that handles decorations, completion sessions, slash menus, Emacs state, and persistence side effects. Suggested direction: preserve the current TextKit 1 architecture but extract focused collaborators such as EditorTextKitFactory, EditorCompletionCoordinator, SlashCommandMenuController, and AutosaveScheduler, with pure parsing/state pieces kept in Core where possible.
acceptance_criteria: RawEditorView/Coordinator shrink to composition and delegate callbacks; autocomplete, slash menu, and autosave behavior each have focused tests; existing editor UI tests and Emacs unit tests continue to pass.
close_reason: RawEditorView 659→473 lines: extracted EditorTextKitFactory, EditorCompletionCoordinator, SlashCommandMenuController (App) with pure decision logic in Core (EditorCompletion session resolution/accept edits; SlashCommandLibrary.menuTrigger + SlashCommand.insertion). 17 new tests. Validated on merged main: arch check OK, strict lint 0, 29 editor + 99 integration tests pass. Autosave wiring (PUNK-j27) untouched. Editor UI tests pending interactive automation permission.

title: Centralize LLM provider settings, defaults, and capabilities
description: Design analysis finding: provider knowledge is duplicated across SettingsView, LLMChatPanel, AppState, and LanguageModelFactory. UserDefaults keys, default models/endpoints, image capability checks, context budgets, and provider selection rules are spread through the App layer and Infra factory. Principle: Pragmatic Programmer DRY as single authoritative representation of knowledge; Code Health Guardian complexity management by making configuration health inspectable. Evidence: @AppStorage keys in Sources/PunkRecordsApp/Views/SettingsView.swift and Sources/PunkRecordsApp/Views/LLMChatPanel.swift, UserDefaults reads in Sources/PunkRecordsApp/App/AppState.swift, defaults in Packages/PunkRecordsInfra/Sources/PunkRecordsInfra/LLMProviders/LanguageModelFactory.swift, and contextBudget(for:) in LLMChatPanel. Suggested direction: add a typed ProviderSettings/ProviderRegistry seam that owns keys, defaults, endpoint parsing, native-image support, and context budgets; views bind to it and factories consume it.
acceptance_criteria: Provider defaults and UserDefaults keys are defined in one typed seam; Settings, chat, note compilation, and LanguageModelFactory consume the same config; tests cover endpoint parsing, default model values, image capability, and context budget lookup.
close_reason: ProviderRegistry (Core) is the single typed authority for provider UserDefaults keys, Keychain accounts, default models/endpoints, endpoint parsing, native-image capability, max image edge, and context budgets. All consumers migrated: SettingsView, thin LLMChatPanel, ChatSessionController, AppState, LanguageModelFactory (Config.fromUserDefaults parses via registry). No persisted key renames; behavior-preserving. 18 new unit tests. Combined gate on merged main: arch check OK, swiftlint --strict 0, 18+99+11 tests pass.

title: Avoid Keychain access during UI test startup
description: UI tests currently trigger a macOS Keychain dialog on the chat/test launch path, which can stall automation before a test actually connects to Anthropic/OpenAI. Identify where provider availability or app startup touches Keychain during UI tests and defer Keychain reads until the user actually selects/connects to a provider that needs credentials.
close_reason: Deferred Keychain reads out of provider availability probing. Remote Anthropic/OpenAI providers now remain selectable without preflight credential reads; Keychain is only read by makeModel when a remote provider is actually used. Validated with LanguageModelFactoryTests, Xcode build, and ChatPanelUITests/testChatButtonTogglesChatPanel via Xcode MCP.

title: Beads Dolt remote has no common ancestor with local DB — sync broken
description: bd dolt push fails: 'no common ancestor' — local embedded Dolt DB and origin's refs/dolt/data have independent histories (classic multi-init divergence per beads docs). All issue data IS safe in git via the passive .beads/issues.jsonl export (committed+pushed on foundationmodels-rehoming). Decide which side is authoritative and run the recovery bd suggests (keep-remote-discard-local, or force local). Do NOT resolve blindly from an agent session — whichever side is discarded loses its unexported history.
notes: Flagged for human decision 2026-07-07: choose authoritative side. Local DB holds all recent issue work and is fully exported to committed .beads/issues.jsonl; remote refs/dolt/data (deb835c) is an independent history with unknown unexported content. Recovery is either force-local (likely correct — local is current) or keep-remote; both discard the other side, so per issue guidance an agent must not pick.

title: VaultPaths attachment flattening leaves '..' substring — VaultPathsTests failing
description: PunkRecordsIntegrationTests: 'Filename with path separators is flattened — no escape from attachments/' fails: sanitizing '../../etc/passwd' yields 'attachments/X/..-..-etc-passwd'. Separators are flattened (no actual traversal possible) but the literal '..' substring survives, violating the test's !path.contains('..') assertion. Decide: strip/replace '..' sequences in the sanitizer (defense in depth, matches test intent) or relax the assertion to check for path traversal segments only. Unrelated to the rehoming; discovered while running integration tests for PUNK-wm9.
close_reason: Legacy LLM stack fully deleted; session path is the sole engine; VaultPaths traversal-substring hardening landed

title: Gate live (API-key) eval suites behind an opt-in env flag + graceful no-key skip
description: The default 'xcodebuild test' invocation runs LiveAgentEvals, LiveSessionAgentEvals and LiveVariantComparison against real APIs whenever a key is in the keychain (~8 min, real cost — LiveVariantComparison alone runs 20 scenarios twice). And absent-key cases record test FAILURES ('Caught error: No OpenAI API key — skipping') instead of skipping. Gate live suites behind e.g. PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1 (swift-testing .enabled(if:) trait) and convert no-key paths to proper skips, so default runs are deterministic/offline and live efficacy runs are an explicit opt-in.
acceptance_criteria: Default test run makes zero network LLM calls and records no failures for missing keys; setting the env flag runs live suites.
close_reason: UI bundle 20/20 green incl. E2E scripted chat turn; live evals gated behind PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1; cmd-4 Ollama, OpenAI base URL wired, note compiler follows chat provider selection

title: Provider UX polish: Ollama shortcut, OpenAI base URL, picker states
description: Leftovers from the rehoming: (1) .anyLanguageModel/Ollama has no keyboard shortcut while cmd-1/2/3 map to Apple/Claude/GPT — add cmd-4; (2) SettingsView captures openAIBaseURL but never persists or uses it — either wire it through LanguageModelFactory config (useful for OpenAI-compatible local servers) or delete the field; (3) chat provider and Settings 'default provider' share the AppStorage key chatProviderID so they silently alias — decide and either rename the setting label or split keys; (4) VaultSettings.defaultLLMProvider vs AppStorage divergence.
acceptance_criteria: cmd-4 selects Ollama; openAIBaseURL either functional end-to-end or removed; provider-default semantics documented and consistent.
close_reason: UI bundle 20/20 green incl. E2E scripted chat turn; live evals gated behind PUNKRECORDS_LIVE_EVALS=1; cmd-4 Ollama, OpenAI base URL wired, note compiler follows chat provider selection

title: Session-path token/latency metrics (replace TokenMetrics.zero)
description: EvalHarness.collectSessionMetrics reports tokens as .zero and per-tool latency 0 because SessionAgentRunner exposes no usage surface. Investigate what AnyLanguageModel exposes (response usage / transcript entries per backend: Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, system). Wire real prompt/completion token counts (falling back to TokenEstimator heuristics when the backend reports none) and wall-clock per-tool latency into TurnMetrics so TokenEfficiencyEvals measure the shipping path. Note: legacy prompt-cache accounting (TokenUsage.cache*) died with AnthropicProvider; restore cache metrics if/when ALM surfaces them.
acceptance_criteria: Session-path eval runs report nonzero token metrics (measured or estimated, flagged which) and nonzero tool latencies; TokenEfficiencyEvals assert against them.
notes: Investigated ALM checkout (commit f22b78e): no first-class token-usage surface on LanguageModelSession.Response or ResponseStream for any backend (only Gemini decodes usageMetadata internally, unexposed). Plan: estimate via Core TokenEstimator — SessionAgentRunner knows each roundPrompt and response text; emit estimated prompt/completion tokens per round (flagged as estimated). Revisit if ALM exposes usage upstream.
close_reason: turnEnd carries estimated usage (TokenEstimator; ALM has no real usage surface); PromptLog-based threading evals landed and caught+fixed a medium-tier ContextBuilder truncation bug

title: Session-semantics evals: instruction folding + context threading regression
description: SessionAgentRunner deliberately passes instructions='' to LanguageModelSession and re-folds instructions + accumulated tool results into each round's prompt (Ollama backend drops session instructions; see commit 79c630a). This is the riskiest correctness area of the rehoming and nothing guards it. Add a prompt-spying LanguageModel test double that records the prompt text it receives each round, plus evals asserting: (1) round 1 prompt contains buildInstructions output; (2) after a tool call, the next round's prompt contains the tool result; (3) grounding: scripted vault excerpts appear in the prompt for each ContextTier.
acceptance_criteria: New eval/unit suite fails if instruction folding or tool-result threading regresses; covers all three context tiers.
notes: ALM at f22b78e includes 'Restore instructions from transcript (#156)' — upstream may have fixed the instructions-dropping behavior that motivated SessionAgentRunner's fold-instructions-into-prompt workaround. When writing these evals, also test whether session instructions now survive on the Ollama backend; if so, the runner could pass instructions: properly and simplify roundPrompt. Keep the folding evals either way — they guard the shipping behavior.
close_reason: turnEnd carries estimated usage (TokenEstimator; ALM has no real usage surface); PromptLog-based threading evals landed and caught+fixed a medium-tier ContextBuilder truncation bug

title: Live Ollama integration test for local-LLM wire path
description: Existing local-provider tests cover only pure encode/decode. Add a gated live integration test that drives the real OllamaProvider against a running server (model discovery + /api/chat completion + native InferenceStats) and auto-skips when no server is reachable.
close_reason: Implemented in OllamaLiveIntegrationTests; 2 tests pass live against running Ollama, Infra suite 138 green.

title: Local LLM providers (Ollama + LM Studio) with model selection and inference stats
description: Add first-class local-LLM support: distinct Ollama (native /api/chat + /api/tags) and LM Studio (OpenAI-compatible /v1) providers. Users validate the connection, list/pick an available model, and see inference stats (time-to-first-token, prefill rate, tokens/sec). Ollama uses native nanosecond metrics; LM Studio falls back to client-side timing. Stats surface in a Settings 'Test connection' benchmark and a per-response footer in chat.
design: Core: add .ollama/.lmStudio to LLMProviderID; InferenceStats Sendable model + pure compute fns (Ollama native + client-side); LocalModel + model-list parsers; ProviderValidationResult; LocalModelProvider sub-protocol. Infra: OllamaProvider + LMStudioProvider actors implementing completeWithTools via chat-completions. App: register providers from UserDefaults endpoints, model selection persisted + live, chat picker, stats footer, Settings test/benchmark UI. Thread stats via LLMToolResponse.stats and AgentEvent.done.
acceptance_criteria: Ollama models list+selectable; chat works against a local model; per-response stats show TTFT/prefill/TPS for Ollama; Settings Test Connection lists models+benchmarks; pure logic unit-tested; build + swiftlint clean.
notes: Rebased onto main (9b8760f) on 2026-06-06: resolved SettingsView conflict by merging Local LLMs tab into main's General/Providers/Editor/Keyboard TabView; AppState auto-merged (vault-open progress + local providers coexist). Build green; Core 181 + Infra 136 unit tests pass; swiftlint 0 serious. Force-pushed; PR #1 CLEAN/MERGEABLE.
close_reason: Implemented on branch feature/local-llm-providers; PR #1 opened. 20 unit tests + bundles green; wire format verified against live Ollama. GUI/LM-Studio end-to-end left as documented manual validation.

title: Emacs editing commands (undo, save, case, transpose)
description: Round out Emacs mode with common editing chords: C-/ (and C-_) undo, C-x C-s save buffer, M-c/M-u/M-l capitalize/upcase/downcase word, M-t transpose words (C-t transpose chars is native), C-o open-line, C-x u undo. Implements the C-x prefix handling needed for C-x C-s / C-x u. Scope is in-buffer editing only — no file-manager or M-x command palette.
acceptance_criteria: With Emacs mode on: C-/ undoes, C-x C-s saves, M-c/M-u/M-l transform the next word's case, M-t transposes words, C-x prefix sequences dispatch correctly.
close_reason: Editing commands done. Core: pure EmacsEdit (caseEdit for M-c/M-u/M-l, transposeWords for M-t; 9 tests). Coordinator: C-/ and C-_ undo, C-x prefix (handleEmacsChord state machine) with C-x C-s save + C-x u undo, M-c/M-u/M-l case, M-t transpose. PillTextView now dispatches raw chords so prefixes resolve in the coordinator. performEmacsCommand switch is now exhaustive over EmacsCommand. Tests: Core 136 green; build + lint clean.

title: Preview: theme alignment with editor (Dracula + dark mode)
description: The preview uses MarkdownUI's stock .gitHub theme, which clashes with the editor's Dracula theme and is not dark-mode adaptive. Build a MarkdownUI Theme mirroring EditorTheme (background, foreground, heading colors, code background, link color) so editor and preview look consistent, honoring the active editor theme and system appearance.
acceptance_criteria: Preview background/text/heading/code/link colors match the active editor theme; readable in both light and dark mode.
close_reason: Added MarkdownUI.Theme.punk(EditorTheme) deriving preview colors from the editor theme (background, text, headings via headingColors, inline code fg/bg, links, blockquote). MarkdownPreviewView now uses .punk(theme) + theme.background instead of .gitHub. UI-only styling — no unit test; needs visual verification in light/dark.

title: Code-fence highlighting for Bash and JSON
description: Extend fenced-code highlighting to bash and json (common in a dev knowledge base; the user's test note has both). Both ship clean SwiftPM support with self-contained highlight queries: tree-sitter-bash v0.23.3, tree-sitter-json v0.24.3 (last json tag before SPM support was dropped).
close_reason: Added tree-sitter-bash 0.23.3 (bash/sh/shell/zsh) and tree-sitter-json 0.24.3 (json/jsonc) — both clean SPM deps with self-contained highlight queries. Bundles verified embedded in the .app. Tests: Infra highlighter suite 20 green.

title: Code-fence highlighting for TypeScript, Rust, C, C++ (+ aliases)
description: Fenced-code syntax highlighting only covers swift/python/javascript. The user's test note uses typescript, rust, cpp fences which fall through to no grammar. Add tree-sitter grammars for TypeScript (inherits JS queries), Rust, C, and C++ (inherits C queries), plus common fence aliases (ts, rs, cpp/c++/cc, js/jsx). TS/C++ need parent+child highlight-query concatenation since their highlights.scm only carry derived rules.
close_reason: Added tree-sitter grammars for TypeScript (+ts/mts/cts), Rust (+rs), C (+h), C++ (+c++/cc/cxx/hpp/...), plus jsx/mjs JS aliases. TS and C++ highlights.scm only carry derived rules, so makeInheritedCodeConfiguration concatenates parent+child highlight queries (JS→TS, C→C++) and compiles them against the child grammar, with graceful fallback to child-only. Grammar bundles verified embedded in the .app with queries. Tests: Infra 111 green (incl. new resolution + merge tests).

title: User-customizable editor themes (theme picker + persistence)
description: The editor now ships a built-in EditorTheme with a .dracula preset (and a system .default), wired through TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter, HybridUXDecorator, and WikilinkDecorator. This issue is the full custom-theme system on top of that foundation: (1) a Settings/preferences theme picker listing built-in presets (Dracula, system default, + room for Solarized/Nord/etc.); (2) persistence of the chosen theme (per-vault or app-wide via RecentVaultsStore/UserDefaults); (3) optionally, user-defined themes — editable palette (background, foreground, heading/strong/emphasis/code/link/marker colors) saved to disk; (4) apply live without reopening the vault. EditorTheme already bundles every color the editor uses, so presets are cheap to add. Currently the editor is hardcoded to .dracula in EditorTextViewRepresentable.
close_reason: Already implemented on main: Settings Editor tab has a theme Picker over EditorThemeCatalog (System Default, Dracula, Solarized Dark/Light, Nord), persisted app-wide via @AppStorage(editor.themeID), applied live via .id(themeID) rebuild in RawEditorView; MarkdownPreviewView themed too. Unit tests: EditorThemeTests, EditorThemeCatalogTests. Optional item (3) user-defined custom palettes was not shipped — file separately if wanted.

title: Editor accessibility + IME verification pass
description: Deferred from W6 (PUNK-gf2). Manual audit: (1) VoiceOver navigation over headings, links, and pill-chip wikilinks; add accessibilityLabel to any attachment-based chips if math/attachments adopt NSTextAttachment. Note: current wikilink/tag pills are attribute-based (not attachments), so VoiceOver reads the underlying source text already. (2) IME composition test with Japanese (kotoeri), Simplified Chinese (pinyin), Korean (2-Set) inside paragraphs with adjacent markdown markers. A guard already skips decoration while hasMarkedText() is true; verify it holds across the three IMEs.

title: Inline + block math rendering ($x^2$, $$...$$)
description: Deferred from W6 (PUNK-gf2). Render inline `$x^2$` and block `$$...$$` LaTeX as NSTextAttachment instances (SwiftMath renders to NSImage/NSView — better fit than LaTeXSwiftUI for TextKit attachments). Source stays editable; preview re-renders on edit. The slash palette already inserts a $$ math block skeleton (the 'Math Block' command). Needs a new SPM dep.

title: Click-to-create for unresolved wikilinks
description: Deferred from W4 (PUNK-6du). A click on an unresolved [[Nonexistent]] pill prompts to create the note (or creates it directly) and opens it. Builds on the existing PillTextView click hit-testing and the vault create-note flow (AppState.createNewNote / repository).
close_reason: Clicking an unresolved [[wikilink]] now prompts (NSAlert) to create a note titled after the link; confirming creates it (H1 + frontmatter title match the link so it resolves afterward) and opens it. Added WikilinkDecorator.ClickAction (.open/.create) + clickAction(at:in:) — unit-tested. PillTextView routes clicks through it; AppState.createNote(titled:) builds on the tested repository.save + FilenameHelpers.uniqueNotePath primitives. 19 wikilink tests pass.

title: Tag autocomplete + click-to-filter
description: Deferred from W4 (PUNK-6du). (1) Autocomplete on `#` once the third char is typed, backed by a tags table from the background indexer. (2) Clicking a #tag pill filters notes by tag (or opens a tag view — tag view itself is a separate issue). Tag pills + hash-derived colors already shipped in W4.
close_reason: Tag autocomplete (#-trigger popover reusing the wikilink completion machinery, vault tags as source) + click-to-filter (#tag pill click sets sidebar query to tag:<name>, SidebarFilter gained tag: prefix support). Pure logic in Core TagAutocomplete + SidebarFilter, tag hit-testing in WikilinkDecorator. Tested: Core 14, Infra WikilinkDecorator 23, Integration SidebarFilter 14.

title: Wikilink autocomplete popover (`[[` trigger)
description: Deferred from W4 (PUNK-6du) visual core. Typing `[[` opens a fuzzy-match popover anchored to the caret, sourced from vault titles (reuse QuickOpenMatcher). Up/Down/Enter selects and inserts `[[Title]]`; Esc dismisses; results update as the user types after `[[`. Anchor the NSPopover/overlay to the caret rect via NSLayoutManager boundingRect(forGlyphRange:in:).
close_reason: Typing [[ opens a caret-anchored completion popover (non-activating NSPanel hosting a SwiftUI list) with fuzzy matches over vault titles (QuickOpenMatcher). ↑/↓ navigate, Enter/Tab insert [[Title]] and place the caret past the closing brackets, Esc dismisses, clicks select. Live-updates as you type after [[. WikilinkAutocomplete (Core) does the pure [[-session detection (10 tests) — handles spaces in titles, stops at ]/newline/|, picks the most-recent opener. PillTextView routes keys via doCommand(by:) while visible. 77 Core tests pass; build clean. NOTE: popover positioning/interaction polish needs a visual check in-app.

title: Code-fence content syntax highlighting via tree-sitter language injection
description:
Acceptance carved out of W3 (PUNK-zp2) so the decorator pass could ship cleanly. Wires per-language tree-sitter grammars (swift, python, javascript at minimum) through Neon's languageProvider so fenced code blocks get colored content based on the info-string language tag. Token-name mapping per language is the main effort.

Approach: add SPM deps for tree-sitter-swift (alex-pinkus), tree-sitter-python, tree-sitter-javascript. Extend TreeSitterMarkdownHighlighter.makeLanguageProvider to map 'swift'/'python'/'javascript'/'js' to their LanguageConfigurations using the existing locateQueriesDirectory fallback. Expand the attribute provider to recognize common capture names (keyword, string, comment, function, type, variable, operator, number).

Beware: tree-sitter/swift-tree-sitter vs ChimeHQ/SwiftTreeSitter identity conflict (the python/javascript grammars depend on tree-sitter/; Neon depends on ChimeHQ/). SPM likely resolves them as the same identity with a warning — verify on first SPM resolve.
close_reason: Fenced code blocks now get syntax-colored content via tree-sitter language injection for swift, python, and javascript (js/py aliases too). Added the three grammar SPM deps; pinned python→0.23.6 and javascript→0.23.1 (tagged releases list src/scanner.c explicitly — master branches use a CWD-relative fileExists that drops the external scanner under SPM and fails to link), and swift→0.7.2-with-generated-files (ships the generated parser.c). languageProvider maps fence languages to LanguageConfigurations via the existing bundle-discovery fallback.
