Theme gained a codeColors palette keyed by capture leading-component (keyword/string/comment/function/type/number/…), wired to the Dracula palette. 17 highlighter tests (incl. grammar loading + color mapping); 104 Infra tests pass.

title: Welcome window + first-run onboarding polish
description:
Polish the first-run experience and the welcome window mentioned in CLAUDE.md.

Why: surfaced during backlog audit — the welcome window exists (per CLAUDE.md "Welcome window (CMD+0) — vault selection/creation via RecentVaultsStore") but the first-run flow is not tracked as a polish issue. New users (incl. wife) deserve a clear path from app-launch to first-typed-note.

Scope:
- Vault picker: "Open existing" / "Create new" / Recent vaults list
- Create-new flow: location picker, optional template ("Empty" / "Sample with starter notes")
- API key prompt with a clearly visible "Skip — use Apple Intelligence" option (so users without API keys can immediately use Foundation Models)
- Quick-tour card on first vault open: keyboard shortcuts, capture hotkey, chat panel, sparse-tree search
- "Show again" toggle for the tour card
- Wired to RecentVaultsStore (already exists)
acceptance_criteria:
- First launch shows the welcome window
- Creating a new vault produces a working vault with optional sample notes
- A user without API keys can open a vault and type a note without seeing any error or modal
- Keyboard shortcut tour card is dismissible and remembers the dismissal

title: Export to HTML / PDF / shareable
description:
Export a note (or selection) to HTML or PDF for sharing outside the vault.

Why: surfaced during backlog audit — Marked-2-style "preview then share" is a baseline expectation. The vault is plain markdown, but recipients usually want a styled artifact. Reuses swift-markdown (already a dep of the editor epic) for canonical rendering.

Scope:
- Render to HTML via swift-markdown's HTMLFormatter (or a custom walker for our extensions like callouts, wikilink chips)
- PDF: render the HTML into a WKWebView, print to PDF via NSPrintOperation, or use Apple's newer PDFKit document generation
- Share Sheet integration: ⌘⇧S opens an export dialog → format → save or share via NSSharingService
- Bundle assets: embedded images via base64 in HTML (single-file export) or accompanying images folder

Deferred: presentation-mode export (slideshow), epub export, Pages/Word interop.
acceptance_criteria:
- File menu: Export → HTML, Export → PDF
- HTML export is a single self-contained file with embedded images
- PDF export matches the visible editor styling (or a print stylesheet)
- Wikilinks render as visible text + tooltip showing the target note name (not active links in exported docs)
- Frontmatter optionally included or stripped via toggle
close_reason: Shipped HTML export with self-contained styling. cmark-gfm-backed renderer in Core (MarkdownHTMLRenderer), File menu 'Export as HTML…' with Cmd+Shift+E, VaultWindow NSSavePanel handler. 10 unit tests for the renderer. PDF export deferred to a follow-up — the renderer's HTML is print-stylesheet ready so PDF can layer on via WKWebView+printToPDF when needed.

title: Autosave + crash recovery for edited notes
description:
Debounced autosave on idle (1–2s after last edit) + crash recovery via sidecar file.

Why: surfaced during backlog audit — the editor exists but there is no tracked autosave / recovery story. A crash mid-edit loses everything since the last manual save. Standard PKM expectation.

Implementation outline:
- Debounce: 1.5s of no edits → write to disk. Plus periodic 30s safety save while typing.
- Recovery: write to `.{filename}.swp` or `{vault}/.punkrecords/recovery/{note-id}.md` first, then atomic rename to target. On launch, scan recovery dir; if any recovery files newer than their target, surface "Recover unsaved changes?" UI per file.
- Dirty state already exists in DocumentEditorViewModel; this issue formalizes the persistence layer.
- Conflict with iCloud Drive sync (PUNK-t4g): atomic write avoids most issues, but document the interaction.
acceptance_criteria:
- Editing a note + force-quitting the app leaves a recovery sidecar
- Relaunch surfaces a recovery dialog listing recoverable notes
- Accepting recovery restores the in-memory unsaved state
- Atomic write semantics — never leave a partial file on crash mid-save
close_reason: Debounced autosave (1.5s idle + 30s periodic backstop) + crash-recovery sidecars at .punkrecords/recovery/{note-id}.md with atomic write-then-rename. Pure Core logic (AutosaveScheduler, RecoveryScan, VaultPaths derivation) unit-tested; FileSystemCrashRecoveryStore actor in Infra; recovery sheet on vault open. Validated on merged main: arch check OK, 18 Core tests + 4 CrashRecoveryTests integration tests pass. Manual-only: sheet visuals and full force-quit→relaunch flow.

title: Image storage convention for vault notes
description:
Define and implement the on-disk convention for images dropped/pasted into a note.

Proposed convention: `{vault}/attachments/{note-slug}/{timestamp-uuid}.{ext}` with the markdown line emitting `![alt](attachments/{note-slug}/{filename})` using vault-relative paths. Alternative considered: per-note `_attachments/` sibling folder (cleaner co-location, more clutter). Pick one and document it.

Why: surfaced during backlog audit — Editor W6 includes math attachments, the chat composer (PUNK-tpy) handles user-attached images, and quick-capture (PUNK-5pr) will probably want screenshot capture. There is no single source of truth for "where do image bytes live in this vault." Decide once.

Scope: convention + writer + collision handling (same filename → uuid suffix) + .gitignore template hint (don't ignore attachments by default).

This is distinct from PUNK-tpy (chat attachments are read-only references to external files; this issue covers content the user creates inside their notes).
acceptance_criteria:
- A document describing the convention exists in the repo (CLAUDE.md addition or docs/conventions.md)
- Drag/paste of an image into a note results in a file at the documented path and a markdown reference using the documented relative path syntax
- Identical filenames in the same note do not overwrite (uuid suffix or sequential disambiguation)
- Existing notes with image links continue to render correctly
close_reason: Shipped VaultPaths in Core/Utilities defining the canonical attachments/{note-stem}/{filename} convention with collision handling, sanitization, and markdown reference formatting. 10 unit tests. CLAUDE.md updated with the contract so future paste/drop handlers + screenshot capture + agentic writes all share a single source of truth.

title: Settings / preferences UI
description:
A central Settings window covering: API key entry per provider (Anthropic, OpenAI) via existing KeychainService; default LLM provider + model (Apple Foundation Models / Claude / GPT); global quick-capture hotkey configuration; default vault path; editor font + theme selection; autosave interval; gitleaks scan toggle.

Why: surfaced during backlog audit — multiple in-flight features (quick-capture hotkey, multi-provider picker, KeychainService) need user-facing config and there is no settings surface tracked. Without this, users edit defaults via terminal or app launch flags.

SwiftUI macOS Settings scene: `Settings { TabView { … } }`. Keychain integration already exists; this issue is the UI on top.
acceptance_criteria:
- ⌘, opens a tabbed Settings window
- General / Providers / Editor / Keyboard tabs at minimum
- API keys stored via KeychainService; never written to disk in plain text
- Provider selection persists across launches
- Hotkey rebinding has a conflict detector against system shortcuts
close_reason: Core delivered on main: SwiftUI Settings scene (cmd-comma) with General/Providers/Editor/Keyboard tabs; Anthropic+OpenAI keys stored via KeychainService (SecureField, never plaintext on disk); provider selection persists via AppStorage; Ollama endpoint/model config with installed-model listing. Not shipped: hotkey rebinding UI (belongs with PUNK-5pr — feature doesn't exist yet), autosave-interval knob (autosave is a fixed 1.5s debounce policy), gitleaks toggle (no gitleaks integration exists), default vault path. Those are speculative until their backing features land.

title: Editor W6: Math attachments + slash commands + AX/IME/perf audit
description:
Polish + the v1 cuts. Acceptable to defer items into v1.1 as they fall out of scope.

Deliverables:
- Inline math `$x^2$` and block math `$$...$$` rendered via SwiftMath or LaTeXSwiftUI as NSTextAttachment instances. Source remains editable; preview re-renders on edit.
- Slash command palette skeleton: `/` at line start (or after whitespace) opens a small popover with a built-in command list (heading, list, code block, table skeleton, callout, math, wikilink, attachment). Extensible structure but no plugin API in v1.
- Accessibility audit: VoiceOver pass on headings, links, attachments; rotor entries; ensure pill-chip attachments expose accessibilityLabel.
- IME testing: manual pass with Japanese (kotoeri), Simplified Chinese (pinyin), Korean (Hangul 2-Set). Common failure point on custom editors — Apple's NSTextInputClient on NSTextView handles this, but verify with composed text spanning markdown markers.
- Performance test: 10MB synthetic markdown file (Faker-generated); 50k-note vault for autocomplete pressure.

Deferred to v1.1 (explicitly not in this week):
- Mermaid / PlantUML / Excalidraw block rendering (ship as plain fenced code blocks for v1)
- Table editor with paste-from-Excel detection
- Properties panel UI on top of frontmatter (the Inspector PUNK-4bz writes callouts; a frontmatter form is separate)
- Block references `[[Note^block-id]]`
- Custom themes
acceptance_criteria:
- Inline + block math renders to readable formula glyphs without breaking source editability
- `/` opens slash command popover with at least 8 built-in commands
- VoiceOver navigates headings, links, and pill-chip wikilinks correctly
- Japanese/Chinese/Korean IME composition works inside a paragraph with adjacent markdown markers
- Editing a 10MB file remains responsive (incremental highlight p99 < 50ms; scroll p99 < 16ms)
- Autocomplete on a 50k-note vault < 50ms p99
close_reason: Delivered the testable core: (1) Slash command palette — '/' at line-start/after-whitespace opens a native NSMenu of 13 built-in commands (headings, lists, task, quote, code, table, divider, wikilink, math, callout); selection replaces the /query and places the caret. SlashCommandLibrary (Core) with trigger detection + fuzzy filter, 14 tests. (2) Performance: decoration was O(doc) per keystroke (175s on 10MB!); now limited to the visible range + buffer via EditorDecorationRange — 10MB decorates in ~3.5ms, well under the 50ms frame budget. Scroll re-decorates the newly visible region. 3 perf tests. (3) IME safety: decoration skips while hasMarkedText() so composition isn't disrupted. Deferred to follow-ups: math attachment rendering (PUNK-1oo), full VoiceOver+IME verification pass (PUNK-cmd).

title: Quick Look generator for vault notes
description:
QuickLookThumbnailing extension renders a vault note's frontmatter + callouts (especially `[!props]`) as a styled preview. Triggered by spacebar in Finder, attachments in Mail, AirDrop, Spotlight results.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, Quick Look is the most underused integration in Mac apps. Letting other apps see vault data without opening PunkRecords is the macOS-native answer to "the format is the API."

Keep the renderer simple: title, frontmatter property table, first paragraph, wikilinks as styled chips (non-clickable in QL view). Match the in-app styling for `[!props]` and `[!note]` callouts so previews look continuous with the app.
acceptance_criteria:
- Spacebar on a vault .md file in Finder shows a styled preview
- Preview renders title, top-level frontmatter properties, and the first paragraph
- Wikilinks visible as styled chips (non-interactive in QL)
- `[!props]` and `[!note]` callouts render with the same look as the main app
- Works on .md files outside the vault too (graceful degradation: no property table, just rendered markdown)

title: Services menu + App Intents (Shortcuts) for system-wide capture/automation
description:
Two complementary system integrations completing the capture trinity:

1. Services menu (NSApp.servicesProvider) — select text in any app → Services → "New PunkRecords note from selection."
2. App Intents (Shortcuts.app) — expose Capture Note, Summarize URL, Search Vault as Shortcut actions. Users build their own automation.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md, the macOS-native capture trinity is status item + global hotkey (PUNK-5pr) + Share Sheet (PUNK-5xp) + Services + Shortcuts. This issue covers the Services + Shortcuts legs; PUNK-5pr and PUNK-5xp cover the other two.

Acceptance leans on Shortcuts.app and the `shortcuts` CLI being available for testing the actions deterministically.
acceptance_criteria:
- Services menu shows "New PunkRecords note from selection" globally (Safari, Mail, Messages, TextEdit, etc.)
- Shortcuts.app surfaces at least three PunkRecords actions: Capture Note, Summarize URL, Search Vault
- Actions are testable via `shortcuts run <name>` from the CLI
- Capture Note action accepts text + optional template name + optional tags

title: DataDetectors-powered date recognition in notes
description:
NSDataDetector runs over note content; recognized dates are underlined on hover with a contextual popover offering: "Schedule" (writes the scheduled property on the nearest heading via the Inspector schema), "Add to Calendar" (EventKit), "Surface in Today smart note."

Inspector date fields accept the same natural-language input via NSDataDetector.

Why: per PunkRecords_Orgmode_Lessons_Translation.md Day 10. macOS has had DataDetectors since 10.5; the framework recognizing dates/addresses/phone numbers in arbitrary text has been hardened for 17 years. The Today smart note picks up scheduled headings via predicate — no new agenda view to build.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dates underlined on hover in the note renderer
- Popover offers: Schedule / Add to Calendar / Surface in Today
- Inspector date fields parse "next Monday" / "tomorrow" / "+2w"
- Today smart note correctly lists headings whose scheduled <= today AND status != done
- "Add to Calendar" creates an EKEvent and writes its identifier back into the heading's property block

title: MCP server exposing vault tools (search/read/create/list/summarize_url)
description:
Lightweight stdio MCP server that exposes the existing AgentTools (VaultSearchTool, ReadDocumentTool, CreateNoteTool, ListDocumentsTool) plus summarize_url to any MCP-aware client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, etc.).

Why: future-proofs the agent surface. Once it exists, you (the developer) can dogfood new workflows from Claude Code without writing dedicated UI for each. Also a credible "AI-native" marker for users who already live in Claude Desktop.

Research ranked this P2; bumping to P1 specifically because (a) it accelerates your own dogfooding velocity, (b) the lift is small given the AgentTool protocol already exists, (c) Swift MCP SDK should be reasonably mature by mid-2026. If the Swift SDK is still rough, ship a Node sidecar.

Implementation: thin wrapper around the existing tools. Read-only by default; opt-in writes (create_note) per session via a config flag. Vault path is the working directory; auth via filesystem permissions.
acceptance_criteria:
- `punkrecords-mcp` binary speaks MCP over stdio
- Claude Desktop can be configured to use it; tools appear in the tool list
- Read tools work out of the box; create_note requires --writable flag
- One-page README documents Claude Desktop and Claude Code setup
close_reason: Merged to main, validated by orchestrator: new standalone SPM package Packages/PunkRecordsMCP producing punkrecords-mcp stdio binary over official modelcontextprotocol/swift-sdk 0.12.1 (probe-verified against Xcode 26.5/Swift 6.3 strict concurrency). Wraps VaultSearchTool/ReadDocumentTool/ListDocumentsTool/CreateNoteTool; summarize_url descoped (backing feature in progress). Read-only by default, create_note gated behind --writable (hidden from tools/list AND rejected defensively). Deliberately NOT wired into project.yml so SwiftNIO/AsyncHTTPClient stay out of the app's build graph. 36 tests green post-merge incl. 2 real subprocess E2E tests over stdio; app build + swiftlint --strict green. README documents Claude Desktop/Claude Code setup. Known limitation documented: no live FS watching — external edits need restart.

title: Daily briefing: scheduled vault summary
description:
Once-per-day generated note summarizing what is interesting / unfinished / worth re-reading in the vault.

Prompt input: notes created or modified in the last 7 days + any notes with open checkboxes / "TODO" / "open thread" markers. Output: 5-bullet brief written to Briefings/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.

Why: NotebookLM's killer demo is "ask the agent what is interesting in my corpus" — no local-first PKM ships this. Cheap and high-perceived-value if it works.

Default provider: Apple Foundation Models (private, free, no network). Upgrade path: explicit "regenerate with Claude/GPT" button.

Scheduling: simple launch-time check (last_briefing_at < 24h ago → generate). Background scheduling via BGAppRefreshTask is a follow-up — not P1.

Risk: speculative — no proof users want this. Hold this issue until at least one of {Mark, wife} explicitly asks for it OR until P0 ships and we have time to experiment. Keep open in backlog.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manual "Generate today's briefing" command produces a Briefings/{date}.md within 15s using Apple Foundation Models
- Auto-generation on app launch when last briefing > 24h old
- Briefing has 5 bullets, each linked to a source note via [[wikilink]]
- User can regenerate with a different provider in one click

title: Web Clipper share extension (forwards URL to summarize_url)
description:
macOS Share Extension that, when invoked from Safari/Chrome/Firefox, sends the current URL to the running PunkRecords main app, which then triggers the same summarize_url flow as the in-app paste path.

Why: ~70% of "web summary" use is "I am reading this — save it," not "I have a URL on my clipboard." A one-click clipper is the natural surface.

Implementation: ShareExtension target (SwiftUI). IPC: main app listens on a local Unix socket (or XPC mach service); extension posts {url, optional selected_text} to it; if the app isn't running, extension launches it via LSOpenURL. If the user selected text before sharing, include it as a hint to bias the summary toward that section.

Bonus: Safari Reader-API integration — if the page is in Reader Mode, prefer Safari's extracted content over our own fetcher (skip Tier 1).
acceptance_criteria:
- Share Extension appears in macOS share sheet from Safari/Chrome/Firefox
- Clicking it produces a note in the vault (same shape as in-app paste path)
- Selected text is recorded as a hint and included in the summary prompt
- Works when main app is closed (cold-launch path)

title: iCloud Drive vault location support
description:
Allow the vault folder to live in ~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~com~punkrecords~vault/ (or any iCloud-backed path) so two devices/users share notes without a custom sync server.

Why: rolling our own E2E sync is months of work; iCloud Drive is free and battle-tested. Section 4 of the research flags subscription fatigue and lock-in as the dominant user complaints; "your vault is a folder in iCloud" is the honest answer.

Work involved:
- Add a known iCloud container to the app entitlements + Info.plist
- Vault picker UI: detect iCloud paths and label them as such
- File watcher (DispatchSource.makeFileSystemObjectSource): handle iCloud download-on-demand (files may appear as placeholders)
- Conflict handling: Apple's "filename (Conflict 1).md" copies surface in a dedicated "Conflicts" view; do NOT auto-merge — last-writer-wins per file with conflicts visible
- Re-index FTS5 on file change
acceptance_criteria:
- Welcome window offers "New Vault in iCloud" alongside local-folder option
- Two Macs signed into the same iCloud see changes propagate within ~30s
- Conflict files appear in a dedicated UI; user can pick a winner
- File watcher handles placeholder-state files without crashing

title: Multi-provider model picker per chat message
description:
UI for selecting provider + model per chat composer turn. Anthropic / OpenAI / Apple Foundation Models already routed in LLMOrchestrator; this issue is the user-facing picker + per-message metadata persistence.

UX: small chip in the chat composer showing current provider+model; click opens a popover with options; keyboard ⌘1 / ⌘2 / ⌘3 for the three providers. Last selection sticks per-window.

Persistence: each chat message records its model in metadata (HTML comment or trailing frontmatter block in the chat transcript file) so re-opening the chat shows which model said what. Important for trust + future "rerun with a different model" workflows.

Why: defends against single-provider lock-in and gives users an escape hatch when one provider regresses or has an outage. Mentioned in research as a P1 differentiator versus Obsidian plugins that lock you into one provider.
acceptance_criteria:
- Provider picker chip visible in chat composer
- ⌘1/2/3 switch provider; choice persists per-window
- Each assistant message displays which model produced it

title: URL ingest: failure-mode handling (PDF, YouTube, paywall, SPA, long pages)
description:
Cover the long tail of URL types so the summarize flow degrades gracefully instead of producing garbage.

Failure modes + handling:
- YouTube/Vimeo: detect via og:type or host match → fetch transcript (YouTube auto-captions) → summarize transcript with &t=Ns timestamp anchors
- PDF (Content-Type: application/pdf or .pdf suffix): route to PDFKit for text + page extraction; cite by page number
- Paywall: short body length + known paywall classnames/domains → surface "paywalled — open in your browser and re-trigger from there"
- Login wall (401/403/redirect to /login): same as paywall + "use WKWebView with your cookies" path
- Infinite scroll: WKWebView fallback with programmatic scroll + DOM mutation wait, cap 30s
- JS-only SPA: automatic via Tier 2 fetcher (handled in WebFetchTool issue)
- Page >50k tokens after extraction: two-stage summarization (per-H2 chunk summaries → meta-summary); store both
- Transcript / comment-thread heuristic: high speaker-name density → switch to "extract participants, decisions, disagreements" prompt template
- Foreign language: detect via lang attr; user setting for "summarize in source lang" vs "translate then summarize"
acceptance_criteria:
- Each failure mode has a detection rule and a deterministic outcome (either successful summary or user-facing explanation)
- Test suite covers each failure path with a fixture URL/HTML
- YouTube transcript path produces timestamp-anchored citations
close_reason: Merged to main (43aac7a). Agent was killed by the spend limit mid-test-writing; orchestrator salvaged and completed. Core detection layer (agent's, fully tested): PDFDetector, VideoDetector (host + og:type), PaywallDetector (short body AND known marker/domain — SPA fixture proves short-alone doesn't misroute), LoginWallDetector (401/403 + SSO hosts + login-ish paths), TranscriptHeuristic (speaker-line density), LongFormChunkPlanner (per-H2 with size-based fallback), ForeignLanguageDetector (html lang + recognizer hint), URLIngestClassifier precedence pdf>video>login>paywall>transcript>longform>article. Infra adapters: PDFKitTextExtractor, YouTubeTranscriptProvider (timedtext parsing), URLIngestSignalExtractor. Orchestrator completed: fixed mid-write chunk-planner tests; fixed real packer bug (per-paragraph token sums drifted over target — now tracks joined-chunk UTF-8 bytes exactly matching TokenEstimator); built RoutedWebSummarySource + wired the live summarize flow (PDF #page=N citations, video &t=Ns citations, typed paywall/login block errors, transcript prompt variant with own promptVersion in frontmatter, chunk plan carried as data); 7 new mocked router-glue tests. Gates: 650 Core / 300 Infra / 140 integration tests, build, lint, arch — all green. Deferred to PUNK follow-up issue: redirect-chain plumbing, two-stage long-form orchestration, live YouTube verification, Content-Type-only PDF detection.

title: In-browser agent chat panel (capture steering + ask-the-vault-about-this-page)
description:
Chat UI inside the browser extension: Chrome sidePanel / Safari popover hosting a minimal streaming chat bound to the app's session agent through the PUNK-ce7 IPC chat endpoint.

WHY (what it accomplishes): (1) capture-time steering — 'file this under Chiba's resonator work' resolves placement while the reader still has context, instead of post-hoc refiling; (2) dedup/connection at read time — 'do I already have notes on this? what did my FDN papers conclude?' against a 60+ paper corpus is the incubator's killer query; (3) synthesis on arrival — 'summarize w.r.t. FLATLINE and link it into the index' as one utterance.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
- The extension sends {prompt, pageContext} where pageContext is the shared core's Readability markdown; the app treats it as a text chat attachment (the TextChatAttachmentHandler path this branch already ships) so the session sees the page without vault pollution.
- Stream AgentEvents over the socket as JSON lines; render textToken as growing bubbles, toolStart/toolEnd as compact chips (mirror LLMChatPanel's bubble-segmentation-on-tool-boundary behavior — narration bubbles then answer, per PUNK-dpl semantics).
- Steered capture = the agent calling create_note through its normal tools; surface the created note path as a clickable confirmation that focuses the note in the app.
- Per-site trust: page content leaves the browser for the configured LLM provider — show a provider badge and a per-domain allow prompt (generalize the Jina consent pattern from PUNK-ibh).
- Keep ALL chat logic in the app; the panel is dumb rendering.
acceptance_criteria: From the side panel: a vault question about the open page streams an answer with tool chips; a steer-capture request creates a note in the right location and links back; per-domain consent gates the first send. Works against the scripted model for a deterministic UI test.

title: Live Preview phase 3: inline image previews (attribute-only, no text mutation)
description:
Render vault image attachments inline in the editor, Obsidian-style. Ships independently of phases 1-2; the hardest Live Preview item on TextKit 1 because TK1 has no clean widget model.

IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE
1. Do NOT insert NSTextAttachment — that mutates the string (undo pollution, dirty-state noise, find/caret offset drift), violating the invariant that decoration never touches source text.
2. Approach: for each image reference whose path matches the vault attachment convention (vault-relative attachments/{note-stem}/{filename}, see VaultPaths in Core/Utilities and the Image attachment convention section of CLAUDE.md): when the caret is OUTSIDE the reference's line, dim the source span and apply an NSParagraphStyle with paragraphSpacing equal to the scaled image height on that paragraph, then draw the image in the created gap from the layout manager (drawBackground) aligned under the line fragment. Caret entering the line removes the spacing and reveals full-contrast source. Attribute-only, undo-safe.
3. Pure function imageRefs(in:scanRange:) returning (range, path, altText) tuples, unit-tested — including percent-encoded spaces (the convention encodes them) and skipping refs inside code fences.
4. Async loading: decode NSImage off the main actor; LRU cache keyed by path+mtime with a small cap; on load completion invalidate layout/display for that paragraph only (reuse phase 0 scoped-invalidation helpers). Cap display height (~320pt), aspect-fit, never upscale.
5. v1 scope: local vault-relative paths only; skip http(s) URLs. The reveal causes line reflow like all folding — keep invalidation scoped.
6. Validate with a note containing ~20 images: scrolling must stay smooth (decoration already restricts to EditorDecorationRange.scanRange).
acceptance_criteria: Images referenced via the vault attachment convention render below their source line when the caret is elsewhere; caret entry collapses the preview and reveals source. Source text is never mutated; undo and find unaffected. imageRefs extraction is pure and unit-tested including percent-encoded paths. Scrolling a 20-image note stays responsive; missing/unloadable images degrade to dimmed source without errors.

title: Pay down SwiftLint warning backlog and make lint a stricter gate
description: Design analysis finding: swiftlint --quiet completes but reports 20 warnings across production, eval, and test code. This is not a functional failure, but persistent advisory warnings weaken the lint gate over time. Principle: Pragmatic Programmer broken windows and Code Health Guardian continuous code health; the cheapest time to keep the codebase clean is before warnings become normalized. Evidence from diagnostic run: force_cast in FSEventStreamWatcher, for_where warnings in SQLiteSearchIndex/FileSystemDocumentRepository/SlashCommand/EvalHarness, shorthand_operator warnings, switch_case_alignment in AgentTool, and line_length warnings in several files. Suggested direction: fix the small warnings, decide whether any rules should remain advisory, then document/run swiftlint --strict as the intended pre-commit or CI quality gate.
acceptance_criteria: swiftlint --quiet reports zero warnings or only explicitly documented exceptions; swiftlint --strict is usable as a pre-commit/CI gate; no behavior changes are introduced while paying down style warnings.
close_reason: SwiftLint backlog 20→0 (6 for_where, 2 switch_case_alignment, 7 line_length, 2 shorthand_operator, 3 force_cast). Three targeted justified inline disables (SwiftUI.Text lacks +=; FSEvents CFArray bridge; NSTextStorage.copy in tests); no .swiftlint.yml exceptions. swiftlint --strict now exits 0 across 196 files and is documented as the pre-commit gate. Verified post-rebase on main: build + 223 Core + 176 Infra + 99 integration tests green. Merged as 7d30d2a.

title: OpenAI native web_search via Responses API
description:
Mirror the Anthropic web_search plumbing on OpenAIProvider so the agent loop
can do web research when the user has chosen the OpenAI default. This is the
provider-native counterpart to PUNK-tb3, not the generic Brave/Tavily
fallback (that stays in PUNK-e5u).

What's needed:

- Recognise that the OpenAI built-in `web_search` tool is only available on
  the **Responses** endpoint (POST /v1/responses), not Chat Completions.
  OpenAIProvider.completeWithTools currently posts to Chat Completions — it
  needs a Responses code path when serverTools contains .webSearch, or a
  full move to Responses for tool-using calls.
- Translate ServerToolConfig.webSearch(maxUses:) into the Responses-tool
  shape: `{"type": "web_search"}` (or `web_search_preview` depending on
  account tier — verify against OpenAI's current docs at implementation
  time).
- Decode the response's output items. Web search results arrive as a
  `web_search_call` item (server-side tool use) and a `message` item with
  URL citation annotations attached to text segments. Map those onto the
  existing ContentBlock.serverToolUse / .serverToolResult cases so AgentLoop
  emits the same .toolStart / .toolEnd events the Anthropic path does — no
  view-layer change required.
- Surface the per-call pricing in the orchestrator's user-visible cost
  estimate (OpenAI charges roughly $25–30 per 1k searches on top of token
  usage at last public pricing).
- Tests:
  - Deterministic AgentLoop tests via ScriptedProvider asserting OpenAI's
    output-item shape round-trips through serverToolUse/Result, matching
    the existing AgentLoopWebSearchTests pattern.
  - Live eval (skips without OPENAI_API_KEY in keychain) that the agent
    actually triggers web_search for a "look up current X" prompt — mirror
    of LiveAgentEvals.liveWebSearchTriggers.

Depends on PUNK-tb3 (done) for the ServerToolConfig + ContentBlock cases.
close_reason: OpenAIProvider.completeWithTools now posts to /v1/responses (kept Chat Completions for complete/stream — Ollama/LM Studio compat). Implements: ServerToolConfig.webSearch → {type: web_search} input tool; ToolDefinition → flat function tool; ContentBlock conversation history → input items with sibling function_call / function_call_output. Decodes the output array: web_search_call → .serverToolUse(query); message output_text → .text + synthesizes .serverToolResult with the model's url_citation annotations (or a 'Search completed' placeholder if none); function_call → .toolUse with stopReason=.toolUse. 10 deterministic OpenAIProviderResponsesTests pass; live eval LiveAgentEvals.liveOpenAIWebSearchTriggers skips cleanly without an OpenAI key, runs end-to-end with one. Settings UI already accepts the OpenAI key.

title: "Save as note" should preserve web citations
description: When a chat reply was answered with web_search citations, the existing Save as Note flow should preserve them — wikilinks for vault references, footnotes or inline (Title)[url] for web sources. NoteCompiler currently treats the response text as opaque; needs to be citation-aware. Depends on web_search landing first so we have a citation shape to design against.
close_reason: saveResponseAsNote now pre-extracts wikilinks and web markdown-links from the source, instructs the LLM to preserve them in the prompt, and post-processes the LLM output: any citation that the model dropped gets recovered into an appended '## Sources' section (web links as [Title](url), wikilinks as [[target|display]]). 4 new tests cover: kept-when-preserved, dropped-web-recovery, dropped-wikilink-recovery, and partial-drop survival. 8 NoteCompiler tests green; build green.

title: Brave (or Tavily) WebSearchTool for providers without a native web_search
description:
Path-B fallback. Path A — provider-native web search — is already done for
Anthropic (PUNK-tb3) and tracked for OpenAI (PUNK-lc2). This issue only
applies to providers that don't have a server-managed web_search tool of
their own (currently FoundationModelsProvider, and any future provider that
ships without one).

What's needed:

- A WebSearchTool conforming to the local AgentTool protocol (not a
  ServerToolConfig). Returns top N URL + title + snippet for a query.
- Pluggable backend: Brave Search API as the default (cheap, keys are easy
  to get); Tavily as an alternative configurable per-vault. API key in
  Keychain, scoped by service identifier so the same key isn't reused
  across services.
- Companion WebFetchTool that pulls a single URL and reduces it to
  reading-friendly markdown (try r.jina.ai first as a hosted readability
  service; fall back to a WKWebView-based extraction if that's blocked).
- Wire-up rule in AgentLoop: when the chosen provider doesn't advertise
  capability X via LLMCapabilities (introduce .nativeWebSearch), the loop
  attaches these local tools so the agent has a path to the web regardless
  of provider.
- Tests:
  - Deterministic: ScriptedProvider exercising WebSearchTool/WebFetchTool
    against a mock URL session (no network in CI).
  - Live (tag .eval, skips without BRAVE_API_KEY in keychain): real query
    returns at least one result with title + URL.

Implement only when there's a concrete need — i.e. we want web research
from FoundationModelsProvider, or we want a vendor-agnostic fallback for
cost-sensitive sessions. Until then this stays open as a placeholder.
